es.summary: Los investigadores proponen una Red de Enmascaramiento Residual que combina redes residuales profundas con refinamiento de características basado en segmentación para el reconocimiento automático de expresiones faciales. El método logra una precisión de última generación en los conjuntos de datos FER2013 y VEMO utilizando mecanismos de atención para enfocarse en características faciales relevantes.

title: XAI for Coding Agent Failures: Transforming Raw Execution Traces into Actionable Insights
summary: The paper presents a systematic explainable AI approach that transforms raw execution traces from LLM-based coding agents into structured, human-interpretable explanations using domain-specific failure taxonomies. A user study with 20 participants demonstrated this method enables developers to identify failure root causes 2.8 times faster and propose correct fixes with 73% higher accuracy.
content:
arXiv:2603.05941v1 Announce Type: cross 
Abstract: Large Language Model (LLM)-based coding agents show promise in automating software development tasks, yet they frequently fail in ways that are difficult for developers to understand and debug. While general-purpose LLMs like GPT can provide ad-hoc explanations of failures, raw execution traces remain challenging to interpret even for experienced developers. We present a systematic explainable AI (XAI) approach that transforms raw agent execution traces into structured, human-interpretable explanations. Our method consists of three key components: (1) a domain-specific failure taxonomy derived from analyzing real agent failures, (2) an automatic annotation system that classifies failures using defined annotation schema, (3) a hybrid explanation generator that produces visual execution flows, natural language explanations, and actionable recommendations. Through a user study with 20 participants (10 technical, 10 non-technical), we demonstrate that our approach enables users to identify failure root causes 2.8 times faster and propose correct fixes with 73% higher accuracy compared to raw execution traces. Importantly, our structured approach outperforms ad-hoc state of the art models explanations by providing consistent, domain-specific insights with integrated visualizations. Our work establishes a framework for systematic agent failure analysis, addressing the critical need for interpretable AI systems in software development workflows

zh.title: 编码代理失败的可解释人工智能：将原始执行跟踪转化为可操作的见解
zh.summary: 该论文提出了一种系统的可解释人工智能方法，使用特定领域的故障分类法将来自基于LLM的编码代理的原始执行跟踪转换为结构化的、人类可解释的解释。一项有20名参与者的用户研究表明，该方法使开发人员能够以2.8倍的速度识别故障根本原因，并以73%的更高准确性提出正确的修复。

fr.title: XAI pour les Défaillances des Agents de Codage : Transformer les Traces d'Exécution Brutes en Informations Exploitables
fr.summary: L'article présente une approche systématique d'IA explicable qui transforme les traces d'exécution brutes des agents de codage basés sur les LLM en explications structurées, interprétables par l'humain, en utilisant des taxonomies de défaillances spécifiques au domaine. Une étude utilisateur avec 20 participants a démontré que cette méthode permet aux développeurs d'identifier les causes racines des défaillances 2,8 fois plus rapidement et de proposer des corrections correctes avec une précision 73 % plus élevée.

de.title: XAI für Kodierungsagent-Fehler: Transformieren von Raw Execution Traces in umsetzbare Erkenntnisse
de.summary: Das Papier präsentiert einen systematischen Ansatz der erklärbaren KI, der rohe Ausführungsspuren von LLM-basierten Kodierungsagenten unter Verwendung domänenspezifischer Fehler-Taxonomien in strukturierte, von Menschen interpretierbare Erklärungen umwandelt. Eine Benutzer-Studie mit 20 Teilnehmern zeigte, dass diese Methode Entwicklern ermöglicht, Fehlerursachen 2,8-mal schneller zu identifizieren und korrekte Behebungen mit 73 % höherer Genauigkeit vorzuschlagen.

es.title: XAI para Fallas de Agentes de Codificación: Transformar Trazas de Ejecución Raw en Insights Accionables
es.summary: El artículo presenta un enfoque sistemático de IA explicable que transforma las trazas de ejecución raw de agentes de codificación basados en LLM en explicaciones estructuradas e interpretables por humanos utilizando taxonomías de falla específicas del dominio. Un estudio de usuario con 20 participantes demostró que este método permite a los desarrolladores identificar las causas raíz de fallas 2,8 veces más rápido y proponer correcciones correctas con una precisión 73% mayor.

title: Energy-Driven Adaptive Visual Token Pruning for Efficient Vision-Language Models
summary: E-AdaPrune introduces an energy-driven adaptive pruning framework for Vision-Language Models that allocates tokens based on image information density determined from singular value spectra. The method achieves consistent improvements on nine benchmarks without additional parameters, including a 5.1% relative boost on reasoning tasks.
content:
arXiv:2603.05950v1 Announce Type: cross 
Abstract: Visual token reduction is critical for accelerating Vision-Language Models (VLMs), yet most existing approaches rely on a fixed budget shared across all inputs, overlooking the substantial variation in image information density. We propose E-AdaPrune, an energy-driven adaptive pruning framework that determines the token budget from the singular value spectrum of the visual features space. By preserving a certain proportion of spectral energy, our method allocates more tokens to information-dense scenes while aggressively compressing redundant ones, without introducing additional learnable parameters. We evaluate E-AdaPrune on nine benchmarks and three VLM backbones, LLaVA-1.5-7B, LLaVA-1.5-13B, and LLaVA-NeXT-8B. Under matched average token budgets, E-AdaPrune consistently yields an average improvement of up to 0.6\%, including a significant +5.1\% relative boost on the MMVet reasoning task. Using randomized singular value decomposition, the additional latency is limited to 8ms per image.

zh.title: 能量驱动的自适应视觉令牌修剪，用于高效的视觉语言模型
zh.summary: E-AdaPrune引入了一种能量驱动的自适应修剪框架用于视觉语言模型，该框架根据从奇异值谱确定的图像信息密度分配令牌。该方法在9个基准测试中取得一致改进，无需额外参数，包括在推理任务上获得5.1%的相对提升。

fr.title: Élagage des Jetons Visuels Adaptatif Piloté par l'Énergie pour des Modèles Vision-Langage Efficaces
fr.summary: E-AdaPrune introduit un cadre d'élagage adaptatif piloté par l'énergie pour les Modèles Vision-Langage qui alloue les jetons en fonction de la densité d'information d'image déterminée par les spectres de valeurs singulières. La méthode réalise des améliorations constantes sur neuf benchmarks sans paramètres supplémentaires, y compris une amélioration relative de 5,1% sur les tâches de raisonnement.

de.title: Energiegetriebenes adaptives Visual Token Pruning für effiziente Vision-Language-Modelle
de.summary: E-AdaPrune führt ein energiegetriebenes adaptives Pruning-Framework für Vision-Language-Modelle ein, das Token basierend auf der Bildinformationsdichte, die aus singulären Wertspektren bestimmt wird, zuweist. Die Methode erzielt konsistente Verbesserungen in neun Benchmarks ohne zusätzliche Parameter, einschließlich einer 5,1%igen relativen Verbesserung bei Reasoning-Aufgaben.

es.title: Poda de Tokens Visuales Adaptativa Impulsada por Energía para Modelos de Visión-Lenguaje Eficientes
es.summary: E-AdaPrune introduce un marco de poda adaptativa impulsada por energía para Modelos de Visión-Lenguaje que asigna tokens según la densidad de información de la imagen determinada por espectros de valores singulares. El método logra mejoras consistentes en nueve benchmarks sin parámetros adicionales, incluyendo un aumento relativo del 5,1% en tareas de razonamiento.

title: Who We Are, Where We Are: Mental Health at the Intersection of Person, Situation, and Large Language Models
summary: Researchers develop interpretable computational models integrating psychological theory with language models to predict well-being and identify mental states from social media data. By combining person-level traits with situational features from psychological frameworks, the approach provides both competitive performance and greater interpretability compared to pure embedding-based methods.
content:
arXiv:2603.05953v1 Announce Type: cross 
Abstract: Mental health is not a fixed trait but a dynamic process shaped by the interplay between individual dispositions and situational contexts. Building on interactionist and constructionist psychological theories, we develop interpretable models to predict well-being and identify adaptive and maladaptive self-states in longitudinal social media data. Our approach integrates person-level psychological traits (e.g., resilience, cognitive distortions, implicit motives) with language-inferred situational features derived from the Situational 8 DIAMONDS framework. We compare these theory-grounded features to embeddings from a psychometrically-informed language model that captures temporal and individual-specific patterns. Results show that our principled, theory-driven features provide competitive performance while offering greater interpretability. Qualitative analyses further highlight the psychological coherence of features most predictive of well-being. These findings underscore the value of integrating computational modeling with psychological theory to assess dynamic mental states in contextually sensitive and human-understandable ways.

zh.title: 我们是谁，我们在哪里：人、情境和大语言模型交叉口处的心理健康
zh.summary: 研究人员开发了可解释的计算模型，将心理学理论与语言模型相结合，以预测福祉并从社交媒体数据中识别心理状态。通过将人格层面的特征与心理学框架中的情境特征相结合，该方法相比纯基于嵌入的方法提供了竞争性的性能和更高的可解释性。

fr.title: Qui Sommes-nous, Où Sommes-nous : Santé Mentale à l'Intersection de la Personne, de la Situation et des Grands Modèles de Langage
fr.summary: Les chercheurs développent des modèles informatiques interprétables intégrant la théorie psychologique avec les modèles de langage pour prédire le bien-être et identifier les états mentaux à partir des données des médias sociaux. En combinant les traits au niveau personnel avec les caractéristiques situationnelles des cadres psychologiques, l'approche fournit à la fois des performances compétitives et une plus grande interprétabilité par rapport aux méthodes purement basées sur les embeddings.

de.title: Wer Wir Sind, Wo Wir Sind: Psychische Gesundheit an der Schnittstelle von Person, Situation und Großen Sprachmodellen
de.summary: Forscher entwickeln interpretierbare Rechenmodelle, die psychologische Theorie mit Sprachmodellen integrieren, um Wohlbefinden vorherzusagen und psychische Zustände aus Daten sozialer Medien zu identifizieren. Durch die Kombination von Merkmalen auf Personenebene mit situativen Merkmalen aus psychologischen Rahmenwerken bietet der Ansatz sowohl wettbewerbsfähige Leistung als auch größere Interpretierbarkeit im Vergleich zu rein einbettungsbasierten Methoden.

es.title: Quiénes Somos, Dónde Estamos: Salud Mental en la Intersección de la Persona, la Situación y los Grandes Modelos de Lenguaje
es.summary: Los investigadores desarrollan modelos computacionales interpretables que integran teoría psicológica con modelos de lenguaje para predecir el bienestar e identificar estados mentales a partir de datos de redes sociales. Al combinar características a nivel de persona con características situacionales de marcos psicológicos, el enfoque proporciona tanto desempeño competitivo como mayor interpretabilidad en comparación con métodos puramente basados en embeddings.

title: Domain-Adaptive Model Merging across Disconnected Modes
summary: The paper introduces DMM, a data-free model merging framework that consolidates knowledge from multiple specialized domain models into one unified model without requiring access to original data. By synthesizing pseudo-data and using distillation, DMM preserves rare critical knowledge while maintaining stability across highly divergent models.
content:
arXiv:2603.05957v1 Announce Type: cross 
Abstract: Learning across domains is challenging when data cannot be centralized due to privacy or heterogeneity, which limits the ability to train a single comprehensive model. Model merging provides an appealing alternative by consolidating knowledge from multiple specialized models into one, avoiding data sharing and reducing retraining cost. In this work, we present DMM, a data-free model merging framework designed to handle highly divergent models. DMM proceeds in three steps. First, domain-specific models are trained independently. Second, models with high similarity are merged using standard techniques to ensure stability. Third, we synthesize pseudo-data from normalization statistics and distill knowledge from divergent models into the merged model through a lightweight refinement guided by these samples. This approach preserves rare but critical knowledge while maintaining stability. Extensive experiments on unimodal and multimodal benchmarks show that DMM achieves state-of-the-art performance over existing merging methods.

zh.title: 跨断开模式的域自适应模型合并
zh.summary: 本文介绍了DMM，一个无数据模型合并框架，将多个专业领域模型的知识整合到一个统一的模型中，无需访问原始数据。通过合成伪数据和使用蒸馏，DMM在保持高度分散模型的稳定性的同时保护稀有关键知识。

fr.title: Fusion de Modèles Adaptatifs au Domaine entre des Modes Déconnectés
fr.summary: L'article introduit DMM, un cadre de fusion de modèles sans données qui consolide les connaissances de plusieurs modèles de domaines spécialisés en un modèle unifié sans nécessiter l'accès aux données d'origine. En synthétisant des pseudo-données et en utilisant la distillation, DMM préserve les connaissances critiques rares tout en maintenant la stabilité à travers des modèles hautement divergents.

de.title: Domain-Adaptive Modellverschmelzung über getrennte Modi
de.summary: Das Papier führt DMM ein, ein datenfreies Modellverschmelzungs-Framework, das Wissen aus mehreren spezialisierten Domain-Modellen in ein einheitliches Modell konsolidiert, ohne Zugriff auf Originaldaten zu benötigen. Durch die Synthese von Pseudo-Daten und die Verwendung von Destillation bewahrt DMM seltenes kritisches Wissen, während es die Stabilität über stark divergente Modelle hinweg aufrechterhält.

es.title: Fusión de Modelos Adaptativos de Dominio en Modos Desconectados
es.summary: El documento introduce DMM, un marco de fusión de modelos sin datos que consolida el conocimiento de múltiples modelos de dominios especializados en un modelo único sin requerir acceso a datos originales. Al sintetizar pseudo-datos y utilizar destilación, DMM preserva el conocimiento crítico raro mientras mantiene la estabilidad en modelos altamente divergentes.

title: Skeleton-to-Image Encoding: Enabling Skeleton Representation Learning via Vision-Pretrained Models
summary: Skeleton-to-Image Encoding (S2I) transforms skeleton sequences into image-like data based on body-part semantics, enabling the use of powerful vision-pretrained models for skeleton representation learning. The method accommodates heterogeneous skeleton formats and demonstrates effectiveness on multiple action recognition datasets with cross-format generalization.
content:
arXiv:2603.05963v1 Announce Type: cross 
Abstract: Recent advances in large-scale pretrained vision models have demonstrated impressive capabilities across a wide range of downstream tasks, including cross-modal and multi-modal scenarios. However, their direct application to 3D human skeleton data remains challenging due to fundamental differences in data format. Moreover, the scarcity of large-scale skeleton datasets and the need to incorporate skeleton data into multi-modal action recognition without introducing additional model branches present significant research opportunities. To address these challenges, we introduce Skeleton-to-Image Encoding (S2I), a novel representation that transforms skeleton sequences into image-like data by partitioning and arranging joints based on body-part semantics and resizing to standardized image dimensions. This encoding enables, for the first time, the use of powerful vision-pretrained models for self-supervised skeleton representation learning, effectively transferring rich visual-domain knowledge to skeleton analysis. While existing skeleton methods often design models tailored to specific, homogeneous skeleton formats, they overlook the structural heterogeneity that naturally arises from diverse data sources. In contrast, our S2I representation offers a unified image-like format that naturally accommodates heterogeneous skeleton data. Extensive experiments on NTU-60, NTU-120, and PKU-MMD demonstrate the effectiveness and generalizability of our method for self-supervised skeleton representation learning, including under challenging cross-format evaluation settings.

zh.title: 骨骼到图像编码：通过视觉预训练模型实现骨骼表示学习
zh.summary: 骨骼到图像编码（S2I）根据身体部分语义将骨骼序列转换为类图像数据，从而能够使用强大的视觉预训练模型进行骨骼表示学习。该方法可以适应异构骨骼格式，并在多个动作识别数据集上展示了有效性，具有跨格式泛化能力。

fr.title: Codage Squelette-vers-Image : Apprentissage de la Représentation Squelettique via les Modèles Pré-entraînés de Vision
fr.summary: Le codage Squelette-vers-Image (S2I) transforme les séquences squelettiques en données semblables à des images en fonction de la sémantique des parties du corps, permettant l'utilisation de puissants modèles pré-entraînés de vision pour l'apprentissage de la représentation squelettique. La méthode s'adapte aux formats squelettiques hétérogènes et démontre son efficacité sur plusieurs ensembles de données de reconnaissance d'actions avec généralisation inter-formats.

de.title: Skelett-zu-Bild-Codierung: Ermöglichung des Skelett-Repräsentationslernens durch vortrainierte Vision-Modelle
de.summary: Die Skelett-zu-Bild-Codierung (S2I) wandelt Skelettsequenzen basierend auf Körperteil-Semantik in bildähnliche Daten um, wodurch die Verwendung leistungsstarker vordefinierter Vision-Modelle für das Skelett-Repräsentationslernen ermöglicht wird. Die Methode passt sich heterogenen Skelettformaten an und zeigt Wirksamkeit bei mehreren Action-Recognition-Datensätzen mit Format-übergreifender Generalisierung.

es.title: Codificación de Esqueleto a Imagen: Habilitación del Aprendizaje de Representación de Esqueleto a través de Modelos Preentrenados de Visión
es.summary: La Codificación de Esqueleto a Imagen (S2I) transforma secuencias de esqueletos en datos similares a imágenes basándose en la semántica de las partes del cuerpo, permitiendo el uso de potentes modelos preentrenados de visión para el aprendizaje de representación de esqueletos. El método se adapta a formatos de esqueleto heterogéneos y demuestra eficacia en múltiples conjuntos de datos de reconocimiento de acciones con generalización entre formatos.

title: Imagine How To Change: Explicit Procedure Modeling for Change Captioning
summary: ProCap reformulates change captioning from static image comparison to dynamic procedure modeling by learning latent procedural dynamics from keyframes. The framework features learnable procedure queries that capture temporal coherence and generate descriptions of visual transformations, demonstrating effectiveness across three datasets.
content:
arXiv:2603.05969v1 Announce Type: cross 
Abstract: Change captioning generates descriptions that explicitly describe the differences between two visually similar images. Existing methods operate on static image pairs, thus ignoring the rich temporal dynamics of the change procedure, which is the key to understand not only what has changed but also how it occurs. We introduce ProCap, a novel framework that reformulates change modeling from static image comparison to dynamic procedure modeling. ProCap features a two-stage design: The first stage trains a procedure encoder to learn the change procedure from a sparse set of keyframes. These keyframes are obtained by automatically generating intermediate frames to make the implicit procedural dynamics explicit and then sampling them to mitigate redundancy. Then the encoder learns to capture the latent dynamics of these keyframes via a caption-conditioned, masked reconstruction task. The second stage integrates this trained encoder within an encoder-decoder model for captioning. Instead of relying on explicit frames from the previous stage -- a process incurring computational overhead and sensitivity to visual noise -- we introduce learnable procedure queries to prompt the encoder for inferring the latent procedure representation, which the decoder then translates into text. The entire model is then trained end-to-end with a captioning loss, ensuring the encoder's output is both temporally coherent and captioning-aligned. Experiments on three datasets demonstrate the effectiveness of ProCap. Code and pre-trained models are available at https://github.com/BlueberryOreo/ProCap

zh.title: 想象如何改变：用于变化描述的显式过程建模
zh.summary: ProCap将变化描述从静态图像比较重新表述为动态过程建模，通过从关键帧学习隐向量过程动力学。该框架具有可学习的过程查询，可以捕捉时间连贯性并生成视觉变换的描述，在三个数据集上展示了有效性。

fr.title: Imaginez Comment Changer : Modélisation Explicite de Procédures pour la Description de Changements
fr.summary: ProCap reformule la description de changements à partir d'une comparaison d'images statiques à une modélisation de procédures dynamiques en apprenant les dynamiques procédurales latentes à partir de cadres clés. Le cadre présente des requêtes de procédures apprenables qui capturent la cohérence temporelle et génèrent des descriptions des transformations visuelles, démontrant l'efficacité sur trois ensembles de données.

de.title: Stellen Sie sich vor, wie man Änderungen vornimmt: Explizite Verfahrensmodellierung zur Änderungsbeschreibung
de.summary: ProCap reformuliert die Änderungsbeschreibung von statischem Bildvergleich zu dynamischer Verfahrensmodellierung, indem latente Verfahrensdynamiken aus Keyframes gelernt werden. Das Framework bietet lernbare Verfahrensabfragen, die zeitliche Kohärenz erfassen und Beschreibungen visueller Transformationen generieren und zeigt Wirksamkeit über drei Datensätze hinweg.

es.title: Imagina Cómo Cambiar: Modelado Explícito de Procedimientos para Descripción de Cambios
es.summary: ProCap reformula la descripción de cambios de comparación de imágenes estáticas a modelado de procedimientos dinámicos aprendiendo dinámicas procedimentales latentes a partir de fotogramas clave. El marco presenta consultas de procedimientos aprendibles que capturan coherencia temporal y generan descripciones de transformaciones visuales, demostrando efectividad en tres conjuntos de datos.

title: Technical Report: Automated Optical Inspection of Surgical Instruments
summary: This technical report addresses automated optical inspection of surgical instruments using deep learning architectures including YOLOv8, ResNet-152, and EfficientNet-b4 on a dataset of 4,414 images. The work identifies critical manufacturing defects such as cracks and rust in Pakistani-made instruments, emphasizing quality assurance for patient safety.
content:
arXiv:2603.05987v1 Announce Type: cross 
Abstract: In the dynamic landscape of modern healthcare, maintaining the highest standards in surgical instruments is critical for clinical success. This report explores the diverse realm of surgical instruments and their associated manufacturing defects, emphasizing their pivotal role in ensuring the safety of surgical procedures. With potentially fatal consequences arising from even minor defects, precision in manufacturing is paramount.The report addresses the identification and rectification of critical defects such as cracks, rust, and structural irregularities. Such scrutiny prevents substantial financial losses for manufacturers and, more crucially, safeguards patient lives. The collaboration with industry leaders Daddy D Pro and Dr. Frigz International, renowned trailblazers in the Sialkot surgical cluster, provides invaluable insights into the analysis of defects in Pakistani-made instruments. This partnership signifies a commitment to advancing automated defect detection methodologies, specifically through the integration of deep learning architectures including YOLOv8, ResNet-152, and EfficientNet-b4, thereby elevating quality standards in the manufacturing process. The scope of this report is to identify various surgical instruments manufactured in Pakistan and analyze their associated defects using a newly developed dataset of 4,414 high-resolution images. By focusing on quality assurance through Automated Optical Inspection (AOI) tools, this document serves as a resource for manufacturers, healthcare professionals, and regulatory bodies. The insights gained contribute to the enhancement of instrument standards, ensuring a more reliable healthcare environment through industry expertise and cutting-edge technology.

zh.title: 技术报告：手术器械的自动光学检测
zh.summary: 本技术报告涉及使用深度学习架构（包括YOLOv8、ResNet-152和EfficientNet-b4）对手术器械进行自动光学检测，数据集包含4,414张图像。该工作识别了巴基斯坦制造的器械中的关键制造缺陷，如裂缝和生锈，强调了患者安全的质量保证。

fr.title: Rapport Technique : Inspection Optique Automatisée des Instruments Chirurgicaux
fr.summary: Ce rapport technique aborde l'inspection optique automatisée des instruments chirurgicaux à l'aide d'architectures d'apprentissage profond comprenant YOLOv8, ResNet-152 et EfficientNet-b4 sur un ensemble de données de 4 414 images. Le travail identifie les défauts de fabrication critiques tels que les fissures et la rouille dans les instruments fabriqués au Pakistan, mettant l'accent sur l'assurance qualité pour la sécurité des patients.

de.title: Technischer Bericht: Automatisierte optische Inspektion von Chirurgieinstrumenten
de.summary: Dieser technische Bericht befasst sich mit der automatisierten optischen Kontrolle von Chirurgieinstrumenten unter Verwendung von Deep-Learning-Architekturen wie YOLOv8, ResNet-152 und EfficientNet-b4 auf einem Datensatz von 4.414 Bildern. Die Arbeit identifiziert kritische Herstellungsmängel wie Risse und Rost in pakistanischen Instrumenten und betont die Qualitätssicherung für die Patientensicherheit.

es.title: Informe Técnico: Inspección Óptica Automatizada de Instrumentos Quirúrgicos
es.summary: Este informe técnico aborda la inspección óptica automatizada de instrumentos quirúrgicos utilizando arquitecturas de aprendizaje profundo que incluyen YOLOv8, ResNet-152 y EfficientNet-b4 en un conjunto de datos de 4.414 imágenes. El trabajo identifica defectos de fabricación críticos como grietas y óxido en instrumentos fabricados en Pakistán, enfatizando la garantía de calidad para la seguridad del paciente.

title: TADPO: Reinforcement Learning Goes Off-road
summary: TADPO introduces a novel policy gradient formulation extending PPO for off-road autonomous driving, leveraging teacher and student trajectories for improved long-horizon planning. The vision-based system demonstrates zero-shot sim-to-real transfer on a full-scale vehicle, representing the first deployment of RL-based policies on a full-scale off-road platform.
content:
arXiv:2603.05995v1 Announce Type: cross 
Abstract: Off-road autonomous driving poses significant challenges such as navigating unmapped, variable terrain with uncertain and diverse dynamics. Addressing these challenges requires effective long-horizon planning and adaptable control. Reinforcement Learning (RL) offers a promising solution by learning control policies directly from interaction. However, because off-road driving is a long-horizon task with low-signal rewards, standard RL methods are challenging to apply in this setting. We introduce TADPO, a novel policy gradient formulation that extends Proximal Policy Optimization (PPO), leveraging off-policy trajectories for teacher guidance and on-policy trajectories for student exploration. Building on this, we develop a vision-based, end-to-end RL system for high-speed off-road driving, capable of navigating extreme slopes and obstacle-rich terrain. We demonstrate our performance in simulation and, importantly, zero-shot sim-to-real transfer on a full-scale off-road vehicle. To our knowledge, this work represents the first deployment of RL-based policies on a full-scale off-road platform.

zh.title: TADPO: 强化学习走向越野环境
zh.summary: TADPO引入了一种新颖的策略梯度公式，扩展了PPO以用于越野自动驾驶，利用教师和学生轨迹进行改进的长期规划。该基于视觉的系统在全尺寸车辆上展示了零样本仿真到现实的转移，代表了强化学习策略在全尺寸越野平台上的首次部署。

fr.title: TADPO : L'Apprentissage par Renforcement se Lance hors-route
fr.summary: TADPO introduit une nouvelle formulation de gradient de politique étendant PPO pour la conduite autonome hors-route, exploitant les trajectoires d'enseignant et d'étudiant pour une planification à long terme améliorée. Le système basé sur la vision démontre un transfert sim-to-real sans apprentissage sur un véhicule en taille réelle, représentant le premier déploiement de politiques basées sur l'RL sur une plateforme hors-route en taille réelle.

de.title: TADPO: Verstärkungslernen im Gelände
de.summary: TADPO führt eine neuartige Policy-Gradient-Formulierung ein, die PPO für das autonome Fahren im Gelände erweitert und Lehrer- und Schülertrajektorien für verbesserte Langfrist-Planung nutzt. Das visionsbasierte System zeigt einen Zero-Shot-Sim-zu-Real-Transfer auf einem vollständigen Fahrzeug und stellt die erste Bereitstellung von RL-basierten Richtlinien auf einer vollständigen Gelände-Plattform dar.

es.title: TADPO: El Aprendizaje por Refuerzo se Va Fuera de Carretera
es.summary: TADPO introduce una nueva formulación de gradiente de política que extiende PPO para conducción autónoma fuera de carretera, aprovechando las trayectorias del profesor y el estudiante para una mejor planificación a largo plazo. El sistema basado en visión demuestra transferencia sim-to-real de cero disparos en un vehículo a escala completa, representando el primer despliegue de políticas basadas en RL en una plataforma fuera de carretera a escala completa.

title: MM-ISTS: Cooperating Irregularly Sampled Time Series Forecasting with Multimodal Vision-Text LLMs
summary: MM-ISTS presents a multimodal framework augmenting irregularly sampled time series forecasting with vision-text large language models, generating informative visual and textual representations. The approach bridges temporal, visual, and textual modalities through cross-modal encoding and adaptive query-based feature extraction for asynchronous observations.
content:
arXiv:2603.05997v1 Announce Type: cross 
Abstract: Irregularly sampled time series (ISTS) are widespread in real-world scenarios, exhibiting asynchronous observations on uneven time intervals across variables. Existing ISTS forecasting methods often solely utilize historical observations to predict future ones while falling short in learning contextual semantics and fine-grained temporal patterns. To address these problems, we achieve MM-ISTS, a multimodal framework augmented by vision-text large language models, that bridges temporal, visual, and textual modalities, facilitating ISTS forecasting. MM-ISTS encompasses a novel two-stage encoding mechanism. In particular, a cross-modal vision-text encoding module is proposed to automatically generate informative visual images and textual data, enabling the capture of intricate temporal patterns and comprehensive contextual understanding, in collaboration with multimodal LLMs (MLLMs). In parallel, ISTS encoding extracts complementary yet enriched temporal features from historical ISTS observations, including multi-view embedding fusion and a temporal-variable encoder. Further, we propose an adaptive query-based feature extractor to compress the learned tokens of MLLMs, filtering out small-scale useful knowledge, which in turn reduces computational costs. In addition, a multimodal alignment module with modality-aware gating is designed to alleviate the modality gap across ISTS, images, and text. Extensive experiments on real data offer insight into the effectiveness of the proposed solutions.

zh.title: MM-ISTS: 采用多模态视觉-文本大语言模型的不规则采样时间序列预测
zh.summary: MM-ISTS展示了一个多模态框架，通过视觉-文本大语言模型增强不规则采样的时间序列预测，生成信息丰富的视觉和文本表示。该方法通过跨模态编码和自适应查询的特征提取，为异步观测桥接了时间、视觉和文本模态。

fr.title: MM-ISTS : Prévision Coopérative des Séries Temporelles Irrégulièrement Échantillonnées avec LLM Multimodaux Vision-Texte
fr.summary: MM-ISTS présente un cadre multimodal augmentant la prévision de séries temporelles irrégulièrement échantillonnées avec des modèles de langage de grande taille vision-texte, générant des représentations visuelles et textuelles informatives. L'approche établit un pont entre les modalités temporelles, visuelles et textuelles grâce à l'encodage multimodal et à l'extraction de caractéristiques adaptative basée sur les requêtes pour les observations asynchrones.

de.title: MM-ISTS: Kooperative Vorhersage unregelmäßig abgetasteter Zeitreihen mit multimodalen Vision-Text-LLMs
de.summary: MM-ISTS präsentiert ein multimodales Framework, das die Vorhersage unregelmäßig abgetasteter Zeitreihen mit Vision-Text-Sprachmodellen erweitert und informative visuelle und textliche Darstellungen generiert. Der Ansatz überbrückt zeitliche, visuelle und textliche Modalitäten durch Cross-Modal-Encoding und adaptive Query-basierte Merkmalsextraktion für asynchrone Beobachtungen.

es.title: MM-ISTS: Pronóstico Cooperativo de Series Temporales Muestreadas Irregularmente con LLM Multimodales de Visión-Texto
es.summary: MM-ISTS presenta un marco multimodal que aumenta el pronóstico de series temporales muestreadas irregularmente con modelos de lenguaje de visión-texto, generando representaciones visuales y textuales informativas. El enfoque conecta modalidades temporales, visuales y textuales a través de codificación de modalidad cruzada y extracción de características adaptativa basada en consultas para observaciones asincrónicas.

title: Restoring Linguistic Grounding in VLA Models via Train-Free Attention Recalibration
summary: The paper identifies linguistic blindness in Vision-Language-Action models where robots prioritize visual cues over language instructions, then proposes Instruction-Guided Attention Recalibration (IGAR), a train-free inference-time mechanism. IGAR rebalances attention to restore language influence without retraining, demonstrated on robotic manipulation tasks and validated on real robots.
content:
arXiv:2603.06001v1 Announce Type: cross 
Abstract: Vision-Language-Action (VLA) models enable robots to perform manipulation tasks directly from natural language instructions and are increasingly viewed as a foundation for generalist robotic policies. However, their reliability under Out-of-Distribution (OOD) instructions remains underexplored. In this paper, we reveal a critical failure mode in which VLA policies continue executing visually plausible actions even when the language instruction contradicts the scene. We refer to this phenomenon as linguistic blindness, where VLA policies prioritize visual priors over instruction semantics during action generation. To systematically analyze this issue, we introduce ICBench, a diagnostic benchmark constructed from the LIBERO dataset that probes language-action coupling by injecting controlled OOD instruction contradictions while keeping the visual environment unchanged. Evaluations on three representative VLA architectures, including Pi0, Pi0.5 and OpenVLA OFT, show that these models frequently succeed at tasks despite logically impossible instructions, revealing a strong visual bias in action generation. To mitigate this issue, we propose Instruction-Guided Attention Recalibration (IGAR), a train-free inference-time mechanism that rebalances attention distributions to restore the influence of language instructions. IGAR operates without retraining or architectural modification and can be directly applied to existing VLA models. Experiments across 30 LIBERO tasks demonstrate that IGAR substantially reduces erroneous execution under OOD contradictory instructions while preserving baseline task performance. We additionally validate the approach on a real Franka robotic arm, where IGAR effectively prevents manipulation triggered by inconsistent instructions.

zh.title: 通过无训练注意力重新校准恢复VLA模型中的语言基础
zh.summary: 该论文发现Vision-Language-Action模型中的语言盲视现象，其中机器人优先考虑视觉线索而非语言指令，然后提出了指令引导的注意力重新校准（IGAR），一种无训练的推理时机制。IGAR重新平衡注意力以恢复语言影响，而无需重新训练，在机器人操作任务上得到演示并在真实机器人上得到验证。

fr.title: Restaurer l'Ancrage Linguistique dans les Modèles VLA par Recalibrage d'Attention sans Entraînement
fr.summary: L'article identifie la cécité linguistique dans les modèles Vision-Language-Action où les robots privilégient les indices visuels aux instructions linguistiques, puis propose Instruction-Guided Attention Recalibration (IGAR), un mécanisme sans entraînement au moment de l'inférence. IGAR rééquilibre l'attention pour restaurer l'influence du langage sans réentraînement, démontré sur des tâches de manipulation robotique et validé sur des robots réels.

de.title: Wiederherstellung der sprachlichen Verankerung in VLA-Modellen durch trainingsfreie Aufmerksamkeitsrekalibrierung
de.summary: Das Papier identifiziert sprachliche Blindheit in Vision-Language-Action-Modellen, bei denen Roboter visuelle Hinweise den Sprachanweisungen vorziehen, und schlägt dann Instruction-Guided Attention Recalibration (IGAR) vor, einen trainingsfreien Inferenzmechanismus. IGAR gleicht die Aufmerksamkeit aus, um den Spracheinfluss ohne Umschulung wiederherzustellen, demonstriert auf Robotic-Manipulation-Tasks und validiert auf echten Robotern.

es.title: Restaurar el Anclaje Lingüístico en Modelos VLA mediante Recalibración de Atención sin Entrenamiento
es.summary: El documento identifica ceguera lingüística en modelos Vision-Language-Action donde los robots priorizan señales visuales sobre instrucciones de lenguaje, luego propone Instruction-Guided Attention Recalibration (IGAR), un mecanismo sin entrenamiento en tiempo de inferencia. IGAR reequilibra la atención para restaurar la influencia del lenguaje sin reentrenamiento, demostrado en tareas de manipulación robótica y validado en robots reales.

title: Demystifying KAN for Vision Tasks: The RepKAN Approach
summary: RepKAN combines CNNs and KANs to create an interpretable architecture for remote sensing image classification that outperforms existing models while providing explicit, physically meaningful reasoning about its predictions.
content:
arXiv:2603.06002v1 Announce Type: cross 
Abstract: Remote sensing image classification is essential for Earth observation, yet standard CNNs and Transformers often function as uninterpretable black-boxes. We propose RepKAN, a novel architecture that integrates the structural efficiency of CNNs with the non-linear representational power of KANs. By utilizing a dual-path design -- Spatial Linear and Spectral Non-linear -- RepKAN enables the autonomous discovery of class-specific spectral fingerprints and physical interaction manifolds. Experimental results on the EuroSAT and NWPU-RESISC45 datasets demonstrate that RepKAN provides explicit physically interpretable reasoning while outperforming state-of-the-art models. These findings indicate that RepKAN holds significant potential to serve as the backbone for future interpretable visual foundation models.

zh.title: 揭秘视觉任务中的KAN：RepKAN方法
zh.summary: RepKAN结合CNN和KAN为遥感图像分类创建了一个可解释的架构，其性能超过现有模型，同时为其预测提供明确的、物理上有意义的推理。

fr.title: Démystifier KAN pour les Tâches de Vision : L'Approche RepKAN
fr.summary: RepKAN combine les CNN et les KAN pour créer une architecture interprétable pour la classification d'images de télédétection qui surpasse les modèles existants tout en fournissant un raisonnement explicite et physiquement significatif sur ses prédictions.

de.title: KAN für Visionsprobleme entmystifizieren: Der RepKAN-Ansatz
de.summary: RepKAN kombiniert CNNs und KANs, um eine interpretierbare Architektur zur Klassifizierung von Fernerkundungsbildern zu schaffen, die bestehende Modelle übertrifft und gleichzeitig explizite, physikalisch aussagekräftige Begründungen für ihre Vorhersagen liefert.

es.title: Desmitificando KAN para Tareas de Visión: El Enfoque RepKAN
es.summary: RepKAN combina CNN y KAN para crear una arquitectura interpretable para la clasificación de imágenes de detección remota que supera los modelos existentes mientras proporciona un razonamiento explícito y físicamente significativo sobre sus predicciones.

title: MASFactory: A Graph-centric Framework for Orchestrating LLM-Based Multi-Agent Systems with Vibe Graphing
summary: MASFactory is a graph-centric framework that simplifies orchestrating LLM-based multi-agent systems by compiling natural-language intent into executable workflows with reusable components and human-in-the-loop interaction.
content:
arXiv:2603.06007v1 Announce Type: cross 
Abstract: Large language model-based (LLM-based) multi-agent systems (MAS) are increasingly used to extend agentic problem solving via role specialization and collaboration. MAS workflows can be naturally modeled as directed computation graphs, where nodes execute agents/sub-workflows and edges encode dependencies and message passing. However, implementing complex graph workflows in current frameworks still requires substantial manual effort, offers limited reuse, and makes it difficult to integrate heterogeneous external context sources. To overcome these limitations, we present MASFactory, a graph-centric framework for orchestrating LLM-based MAS. It introduces Vibe Graphing, a human-in-the-loop approach that compiles natural-language intent into an editable workflow specification and then into an executable graph. In addition, the framework provides reusable components and pluggable context integration, as well as a visualizer for topology preview, runtime tracing, and human-in-the-loop interaction. We evaluate MASFactory on seven public benchmarks, validating both reproduction consistency for representative MAS methods and the effectiveness of Vibe Graphing. Our code (https://github.com/BUPT-GAMMA/MASFactory) and video (https://youtu.be/ANynzVfY32k) are publicly available.

zh.title: MASFactory：用于编排LLM多代理系统的图中心框架
zh.summary: MASFactory是一个以图为中心的框架，通过将自然语言意图编译为具有可重用组件和人机交互的可执行工作流，简化了LLM多代理系统的编排。

fr.title: MASFactory : Un Framework Centré sur le Graphe pour Orchestrer les Systèmes Multi-Agents Basés sur LLM
fr.summary: MASFactory est un framework centré sur le graphe qui simplifie l'orchestration des systèmes multi-agents basés sur LLM en compilant l'intention en langage naturel en workflows exécutables avec des composants réutilisables et une interaction homme-machine.

de.title: MASFactory: Ein Graph-zentriertes Framework zur Orchestrierung von LLM-basierten Multi-Agent-Systemen
de.summary: MASFactory ist ein graph-zentriertes Framework, das die Orchestrierung von LLM-basierten Multi-Agent-Systemen vereinfacht, indem es natürlichsprachliche Absichten in ausführbare Workflows mit wiederverwendbaren Komponenten und Human-in-the-Loop-Interaktion kompiliert.

es.title: MASFactory: Un Marco Centrado en Grafos para Orquestar Sistemas Multi-Agente Basados en LLM
es.summary: MASFactory es un marco centrado en grafos que simplifica la orquestación de sistemas multi-agente basados en LLM al compilar la intención en lenguaje natural en flujos de trabajo ejecutables con componentes reutilizables e interacción humano-máquina.

title: Sensitivity-Aware Retrieval-Augmented Intent Clarification
summary: Researchers developed methods to protect sensitive information in retrieval-augmented conversational agents while maintaining utility for intent clarification in domains like healthcare and legal search.
content:
arXiv:2603.06025v1 Announce Type: cross 
Abstract: In conversational search systems, a key component is to determine and clarify the intent behind complex queries. We view intent clarification in light of the exploratory search paradigm, where users, through an iterative, evolving process of selection, exploration and retrieval, transform a visceral or conscious need into a formalized one. Augmenting the clarification component with a retrieval step (retrieval-augmented intent clarification) can seriously enhance clarification performance, especially in domains where Large Language Models (LLMs) lack parametric knowledge. However, in more sensitive domains, such as healthcare, government (e.g. FOIA search) or legal contexts, the retrieval database may contain sensitive information that needs protection. In this paper, we explore the research challenge of developing a retrieval-augmented conversational agent that can act as a mediator and gatekeeper for the sensitive collection. To do that, we also need to know what we are protecting and against what. We propose to tackle this research challenge in three steps: 1) define an attack model, 2) design sensitivity-aware defenses on the retrieval level and 3) develop evaluation methods to measure the trade-off between the level of protection and the system's utility.

zh.title: 敏感信息感知的检索增强型意图澄清
zh.summary: 研究人员开发了方法来保护检索增强型对话代理中的敏感信息，同时在医疗和法律搜索等领域中保持意图澄清的有用性。

fr.title: Clarification d'intention augmentée par récupération sensible à la sensibilité
fr.summary: Les chercheurs ont développé des méthodes pour protéger les informations sensibles dans les agents conversationnels augmentés par la récupération tout en maintenant l'utilité de la clarification d'intention dans des domaines comme la recherche en santé et le droit.

de.title: Sensitivitätsbewusste, abrufgestützte Intentionsklärung
de.summary: Forscher entwickelten Methoden, um sensible Informationen in abrufgestützten Konversationsagenten zu schützen und gleichzeitig die Nützlichkeit für die Intentionsklärung in Bereichen wie Gesundheitswesen und Rechtssuche zu bewahren.

es.title: Aclaración de intención aumentada por recuperación consciente de sensibilidad
es.summary: Los investigadores desarrollaron métodos para proteger información sensible en agentes conversacionales aumentados por recuperación mientras se mantiene la utilidad para aclaración de intención en dominios como búsqueda de salud y legal.

title: Probing Visual Concepts in Lightweight Vision-Language Models for Automated Driving
summary: Analysis of vision-language models for autonomous driving revealed two failure modes: perceptual failures where visual information isn't encoded, and cognitive failures where visual information isn't properly aligned with language understanding.
content:
arXiv:2603.06054v1 Announce Type: cross 
Abstract: The use of Vision-Language Models (VLMs) in automated driving applications is becoming increasingly common, with the aim of leveraging their reasoning and generalisation capabilities to handle long tail scenarios. However, these models often fail on simple visual questions that are highly relevant to automated driving, and the reasons behind these failures remain poorly understood. In this work, we examine the intermediate activations of VLMs and assess the extent to which specific visual concepts are linearly encoded, with the goal of identifying bottlenecks in the flow of visual information. Specifically, we create counterfactual image sets that differ only in a targeted visual concept and then train linear probes to distinguish between them using the activations of four state-of-the-art (SOTA) VLMs. Our results show that concepts such as the presence of an object or agent in a scene are explicitly and linearly encoded, whereas other spatial visual concepts, such as the orientation of an object or agent, are only implicitly encoded by the spatial structure retained by the vision encoder. In parallel, we observe that in certain cases, even when a concept is linearly encoded in the model's activations, the model still fails to answer correctly. This leads us to identify two failure modes. The first is perceptual failure, where the visual information required to answer a question is not linearly encoded in the model's activations. The second is cognitive failure, where the visual information is present but the model fails to align it correctly with language semantics. Finally, we show that increasing the distance of the object in question quickly degrades the linear separability of the corresponding visual concept. Overall, our findings improve our understanding of failure cases in VLMs on simple visual tasks that are highly relevant to automated driving.

zh.title: 轻量级视觉-语言模型在自动驾驶中的视觉概念探测
zh.summary: 对自动驾驶视觉-语言模型的分析揭示了两种失败模式：感知失败（视觉信息未被编码）和认知失败（视觉信息与语言理解不当对齐）。

fr.title: Sondage des concepts visuels dans les modèles légers de vision-langage pour la conduite automatisée
fr.summary: L'analyse des modèles de vision-langage pour la conduite autonome a révélé deux modes de défaillance : les défaillances perceptuelles où les informations visuelles ne sont pas codées, et les défaillances cognitives où les informations visuelles ne sont pas correctement alignées avec la compréhension du langage.

de.title: Sondierung visueller Konzepte in leichten Vision-Sprache-Modellen für automatisiertes Fahren
de.summary: Die Analyse von Vision-Sprache-Modellen für autonomes Fahren offenbarte zwei Fehlermodi: Wahrnehmungsausfälle, bei denen visuelle Informationen nicht kodiert werden, und kognitive Ausfälle, bei denen visuelle Informationen nicht richtig mit dem Sprachverständnis ausgerichtet sind.

es.title: Sondeo de conceptos visuales en modelos ligeros de visión-lenguaje para conducción automatizada
es.summary: El análisis de modelos de visión-lenguaje para conducción autónoma reveló dos modos de fallo: fallos perceptivos donde la información visual no se codifica, y fallos cognitivos donde la información visual no se alinea adecuadamente con la comprensión del lenguaje.

title: TempoSyncDiff: Distilled Temporally-Consistent Diffusion for Low-Latency Audio-Driven Talking Head Generation
summary: TempoSyncDiff enables efficient, low-latency audio-driven talking head generation through distilled diffusion, achieving temporal consistency and stable emotional expression with significantly reduced inference time.
content:
arXiv:2603.06057v1 Announce Type: cross 
Abstract: Diffusion models have recently advanced photorealistic human synthesis, although practical talking-head generation (THG) remains constrained by high inference latency, temporal instability such as flicker and identity drift, and imperfect audio-visual alignment under challenging speech conditions. This paper introduces TempoSyncDiff, a reference-conditioned latent diffusion framework that explores few-step inference for efficient audio-driven talking-head generation. The approach adopts a teacher-student distillation formulation in which a diffusion teacher trained with a standard noise prediction objective guides a lightweight student denoiser capable of operating with significantly fewer inference steps to improve generation stability. The framework incorporates identity anchoring and temporal regularization designed to mitigate identity drift and frame-to-frame flicker during synthesis, while viseme-based audio conditioning provides coarse lip motion control. Experiments on the LRS3 dataset report denoising-stage component-level metrics relative to VAE reconstructions and preliminary latency characterization, including CPU-only and edge computing measurements and feasibility estimates for edge deployment. The results suggest that distilled diffusion models can retain much of the reconstruction behaviour of a stronger teacher while enabling substantially lower latency inference. The study is positioned as an initial step toward practical diffusion-based talking-head generation under constrained computational settings. GitHub: https://mazumdarsoumya.github.io/TempoSyncDiff

zh.title: TempoSyncDiff：用于低延迟音频驱动说话头生成的蒸馏时间一致性扩散
zh.summary: TempoSyncDiff通过蒸馏扩散实现高效、低延迟的音频驱动说话头生成，实现时间一致性和稳定的情感表达，同时显著减少推理时间。

fr.title: TempoSyncDiff : Diffusion temporellement cohérente distillée pour la génération de têtes parlantes audio-pilotées à faible latence
fr.summary: TempoSyncDiff permet une génération efficace de têtes parlantes pilotées par audio à faible latence grâce à la diffusion distillée, réalisant une cohérence temporelle et une expression émotionnelle stable avec un temps d'inférence considérablement réduit.

de.title: TempoSyncDiff: Destillierte zeitlich-konsistente Diffusion für Sprech-Kopf-Generierung mit niedriger Latenz
de.summary: TempoSyncDiff ermöglicht effiziente, latenzarme audiogesteuerte Sprech-Kopf-Generierung durch destillierte Diffusion und erreicht zeitliche Konsistenz und stabilen emotionalen Ausdruck mit deutlich reduzierter Inferenzzeit.

es.title: TempoSyncDiff: Difusión temporalmente consistente destilada para generación de cabeza parlante impulsada por audio de baja latencia
es.summary: TempoSyncDiff permite la generación eficiente de cabeza parlante impulsada por audio de baja latencia a través de difusión destilada, logrando consistencia temporal y expresión emocional estable con tiempo de inferencia significativamente reducido.

title: Evaluating Austrian A-Level German Essays with Large Language Models for Automated Essay Scoring
summary: Evaluation of state-of-the-art open-weight LLMs for grading Austrian German essays showed limited real-world applicability, with models achieving only 32.8% agreement on final grades despite using standardized rubrics.
content:
arXiv:2603.06066v1 Announce Type: cross 
Abstract: Automated Essay Scoring (AES) has been explored for decades with the goal to support teachers by reducing grading workload and mitigating subjective biases. While early systems relied on handcrafted features and statistical models, recent advances in Large Language Models (LLMs) have made it possible to evaluate student writing with unprecedented flexibility. This paper investigates the application of state-of-the-art open-weight LLMs for the grading of Austrian A-level German texts, with a particular focus on rubric-based evaluation. A dataset of 101 anonymised student exams across three text types was processed and evaluated. Four LLMs, DeepSeek-R1 32b, Qwen3 30b, Mixtral 8x7b and LLama3.3 70b, were evaluated with different contexts and prompting strategies. The LLMs were able to reach a maximum of 40.6% agreement with the human rater in the rubric-provided sub-dimensions, and only 32.8% of final grades matched the ones given by a human expert. The results indicate that even though smaller models are able to use standardised rubrics for German essay grading, they are not accurate enough to be used in a real-world grading environment.

zh.title: 使用大型语言模型评估奥地利A级德语论文的自动评分
zh.summary: 对最先进的开源大型语言模型的评估表明，用于评分奥地利德语论文的模型在实际应用中的适用性有限，尽管使用了标准化的评分标准，但模型对最终成绩的一致性仅为32.8%。

fr.title: Évaluation des essais d'allemand de niveau A autrichien avec des modèles de langage volumineux pour la notation automatique des essais
fr.summary: L'évaluation des modèles de langage ouverts les plus avancés pour la notation des essais allemands autrichiens a montré une applicabilité réelle limitée, les modèles n'obtenant qu'un accord de 32,8% sur les notes finales malgré l'utilisation de critères normalisés.

de.title: Bewertung österreichischer A-Level-Deutschaufsätze mit großen Sprachmodellen für automatisierte Aufsatzbewertung
de.summary: Eine Evaluierung modernster Open-Source-Sprachmodelle zur Bewertung österreichischer deutschsprachiger Aufsätze zeigte begrenzte praktische Anwendbarkeit, wobei Modelle trotz Verwendung standardisierter Bewertungskriterien nur 32,8% Übereinstimmung bei den Endnoten erreichten.

es.title: Evaluación de ensayos de alemán de nivel A austriaco con modelos de lenguaje grandes para calificación automática de ensayos
es.summary: La evaluación de modelos de lenguaje abiertos de última generación para calificar ensayos de alemán austriaco mostró aplicabilidad limitada en el mundo real, con modelos logrando solo un 32,8% de acuerdo en las calificaciones finales a pesar de usar criterios estandarizados.

title: Text-Driven Emotionally Continuous Talking Face Generation
summary: A novel framework (TIE-TFG) generates realistic talking faces that reflect continuously changing emotions in response to text input, creating more natural and emotionally nuanced synthetic videos.
content:
arXiv:2603.06071v1 Announce Type: cross 
Abstract: Talking Face Generation (TFG) strives to create realistic and emotionally expressive digital faces. While previous TFG works have mastered the creation of naturalistic facial movements, they typically express a fixed target emotion in synthetic videos and lack the ability to exhibit continuously changing and natural expressions like humans do when conveying information. To synthesize realistic videos, we propose a novel task called Emotionally Continuous Talking Face Generation (EC-TFG), which takes a text segment and an emotion description with varying emotions as driving data, aiming to generate a video where the person speaks the text while reflecting the emotional changes within the description. Alongside this, we introduce a customized model, i.e., Temporal-Intensive Emotion Modulated Talking Face Generation (TIE-TFG), which innovatively manages dynamic emotional variations by employing Temporal-Intensive Emotion Fluctuation Modeling, allowing it to provide emotion variation sequences corresponding to the input text to drive continuous facial expression changes in synthesized videos. Extensive evaluations demonstrate our method's exceptional ability to produce smooth emotion transitions and uphold high-quality visuals and motion authenticity across diverse emotional states.

zh.title: 文本驱动的情感连续说话人脸生成
zh.summary: 一个新颖的框架（TIE-TFG）生成逼真的说话人脸，能够在响应文本输入时反映持续变化的情感，创建更自然和情感细致的合成视频。

fr.title: Génération de visages parlants émotionnellement continus pilotée par le texte
fr.summary: Un cadre novateur (TIE-TFG) génère des visages parlants réalistes qui reflètent les émotions en constante évolution en réponse à l'entrée textuelle, créant des vidéos synthétiques plus naturelles et nuancées sur le plan émotionnel.

de.title: Textgesteuerte emotional kontinuierliche Talking-Face-Generierung
de.summary: Ein neuartiges Framework (TIE-TFG) generiert realistische sprechende Gesichter, die kontinuierlich wechselnde Emotionen als Reaktion auf Texteingaben widerspiegeln und natürlichere, emotional differenzierte synthetische Videos schaffen.

es.title: Generación de rostros parlantes emocionalmente continuos impulsada por texto
es.summary: Un marco novedoso (TIE-TFG) genera rostros parlantes realistas que reflejan emociones continuamente cambiantes en respuesta a la entrada de texto, creando videos sintéticos más naturales y matizados emocionalmente.

title: Lifelong Embodied Navigation Learning
summary: Uni-Walker addresses catastrophic forgetting in embodied navigation by decoupling task-shared and task-specific knowledge, enabling agents to continually learn new navigation skills across diverse environments.
content:
arXiv:2603.06073v1 Announce Type: cross 
Abstract: Embodied navigation agents powered by large language models have shown strong performance on individual tasks but struggle to continually acquire new navigation skills, which suffer from catastrophic forgetting. We formalize this challenge as lifelong embodied navigation learning (LENL), where an agent is required to adapt to a sequence of navigation tasks spanning multiple scenes and diverse user instruction styles, while retaining previously learned knowledge. To tackle this problem, we propose Uni-Walker, a lifelong embodied navigation framework that decouples navigation knowledge into task-shared and task-specific components with Decoder Extension LoRA (DE-LoRA). To learn the shared knowledge, we design a knowledge inheritance strategy and an experts co-activation strategy to facilitate shared knowledge transfer and refinement across multiple navigation tasks. To learn the specific knowledge, we propose an expert subspace orthogonality constraint together and a navigation-specific chain-of-thought reasoning mechanism to capture specific knowledge and enhance instruction-style understanding. Extensive experiments demonstrate the superiority of Uni-Walker for building universal navigation agents with lifelong learning.

zh.title: 终身具身导航学习
zh.summary: Uni-Walker通过分离任务共享和任务特定的知识来解决具身导航中的灾难性遗忘问题，使代理能够在不同的环境中不断学习新的导航技能。

fr.title: Apprentissage de la navigation incarnée tout au long de la vie
fr.summary: Uni-Walker résout l'oubli catastrophique en navigation incarnée en découplant les connaissances partagées des tâches et spécifiques aux tâches, permettant aux agents d'apprendre continuellement de nouvelles compétences de navigation dans divers environnements.

de.title: Lebenslanges verkörpertes Navigationserlernen
de.summary: Uni-Walker behandelt katastrophales Vergessen in der verkörperten Navigation durch das Entkoppeln von task-gemeinsamen und task-spezifischen Kenntnissen, sodass Agenten kontinuierlich neue Navigationsfähigkeiten in verschiedenen Umgebungen erlernen können.

es.title: Aprendizaje de navegación encarnada de por vida
es.summary: Uni-Walker aborda el olvido catastrófico en la navegación encarnada al desacoplar el conocimiento compartido por tareas y específico de tareas, permitiendo que los agentes aprendan continuamente nuevas habilidades de navegación en diversos entornos.

title: StreamVoiceAnon+: Emotion-Preserving Streaming Speaker Anonymization via Frame-Level Acoustic Distillation
summary: StreamVoiceAnon+ preserves emotional content during real-time speaker anonymization through frame-level acoustic distillation, achieving 49.2% emotion preservation while maintaining privacy protection.
content:
arXiv:2603.06079v1 Announce Type: cross 
Abstract: We address the challenge of preserving emotional content in streaming speaker anonymization (SA). Neural audio codec language models trained for audio continuation tend to degrade source emotion: content tokens discard emotional information, and the model defaults to dominant acoustic patterns rather than preserving paralinguistic attributes. We propose supervised finetuning with neutral-emotion utterance pairs from the same speaker, combined with frame-level emotion distillation on acoustic token hidden states. All modifications are confined to finetuning, which takes less than 2 hours on 4 GPUs and adds zero inference latency overhead, while maintaining a competitive 180ms streaming latency. On the VoicePrivacy 2024 protocol, our approach achieves a 49.2% UAR (emotion preservation) with 5.77% WER (intelligibility), a +24% relative UAR improvement over the baseline (39.7%->49.2%) and +10% over the emotion-prompt variant (44.6% UAR), while maintaining strong privacy (EER 49.0%). Demo and code are available: https://anonymous3842031239.github.io/

zh.title: StreamVoiceAnon+：基于帧级声学蒸馏的情感保留流式说话人匿名化
zh.summary: StreamVoiceAnon+ 通过帧级声学蒸馏在实时说话人匿名化中保留情感内容，实现了49.2%的情感保留率，同时保护隐私。

fr.title: StreamVoiceAnon+ : Anonymisation du locuteur en continu préservant les émotions via distillation acoustique au niveau des trames
fr.summary: StreamVoiceAnon+ préserve le contenu émotionnel lors de l'anonymisation du locuteur en temps réel grâce à la distillation acoustique au niveau des trames, réalisant une préservation des émotions de 49,2% tout en maintenant la protection de la vie privée.

de.title: StreamVoiceAnon+: Emotionserhaltende Streaming-Sprecheranonymisierung durch Frame-Level-Akustik-Destillation
de.summary: StreamVoiceAnon+ erhält emotionale Inhalte während der Echtzeit-Sprecheranonymisierung durch Frame-Level-Akustik-Destillation, erreicht eine Emotionserhaltungsrate von 49,2% und wahrt gleichzeitig den Datenschutz.

es.title: StreamVoiceAnon+: Anonimización de locutor en tiempo real que preserva emociones mediante destilación acústica a nivel de fotogramas
es.summary: StreamVoiceAnon+ preserva contenido emocional durante la anonimización de locutor en tiempo real mediante destilación acústica a nivel de fotogramas, logrando una preservación del 49,2% de emociones mientras mantiene la protección de privacidad.

title: Experiences Build Characters: The Linguistic Origins and Functional Impact of LLM Personality
summary: Study reveals that domain-specific pre-training shapes distinct LLM personalities, with findings showing that models with reduced social traits perform better on complex reasoning tasks.
content:
arXiv:2603.06088v1 Announce Type: cross 
Abstract: Human problem-solving is enriched by a diversity of styles and personality traits, yet the development of Large Language Models (LLMs) has largely prioritized uniform performance benchmarks that favour specific behavioural tendencies such as assertiveness. To investigate how diverse experiences shape machine personality and influence problem-solving, this study employs continued pre-training to expose models to domain-specific texts in an unsupervised manner, simulating the accumulation of experience. By adapting the Big Five framework via the Machine Personality Inventory (MPI), we quantify the personality traits of these model variants and analyse their relationship to linguistic style and reasoning behaviour. The findings reveal that model competence is bimodal, peaking at "Expressive Generalists" and "Suppressed Specialists," while identifying a "Suppression Advantage" where reduced social traits enhance complex reasoning performance. This study further establishes a causal link between training data linguistics, such as imperative frequency, and lexical diversity, providing a roadmap for "Personality Engineering".

zh.title: 经验塑造性格：大语言模型人格的语言起源与功能影响
zh.summary: 研究表明，特定领域的预训练塑造了不同的大语言模型人格，研究发现具有减少社交特征的模型在复杂推理任务中表现更好。

fr.title: Les expériences construisent les caractères : origines linguistiques et impact fonctionnel de la personnalité des LLM
fr.summary: L'étude révèle que le préentraînement spécifique au domaine façonne des personnalités distinctes des LLM, les résultats montrant que les modèles avec des traits sociaux réduits performent mieux sur les tâches de raisonnement complexe.

de.title: Erfahrungen prägen Charaktere: Die sprachlichen Ursprünge und funktionalen Auswirkungen der LLM-Persönlichkeit
de.summary: Die Studie zeigt, dass domänenspezifisches Vortraining unterschiedliche LLM-Persönlichkeiten prägt, mit Ergebnissen, die zeigen, dass Modelle mit reduzierten sozialen Merkmalen bei komplexen Denkaufgaben besser abschneiden.

es.title: Las experiencias construyen personajes: Los orígenes lingüísticos e impacto funcional de la personalidad de los LLM
es.summary: El estudio revela que el preentrenamiento específico de dominio forma distintas personalidades de LLM, con hallazgos que muestran que los modelos con rasgos sociales reducidos funcionan mejor en tareas de razonamiento complejo.

title: Making Implicit Premises Explicit in Logical Understanding of Enthymemes
summary: A pipeline combining LLMs and neuro-symbolic reasoning identifies and formalizes implicit premises in enthymematic arguments, enabling automatic detection of logical entailment in real-world discourse.
content:
arXiv:2603.06114v1 Announce Type: cross 
Abstract: Real-world arguments in text and dialogues are normally enthymemes (i.e. some of their premises and/or claims are implicit). Natural language processing (NLP) methods for handling enthymemes can potentially identify enthymemes in text but they do not decode their underlying logic, whereas logic-based approaches for handling them assume a knowledgebase with sufficient formulae that can be used to decode them via abduction. There is therefore a lack of a systematic method for translating textual components of an enthymeme into a logical argument and generating the logical formulae required for their decoding, and thereby showing logical entailment. To address this, we propose a pipeline that integrates: (1) a large language model (LLM) to generate intermediate implicit premises based on the explicit premise and claim; (2) another LLM to translate the natural language into logical formulas; and (3) a neuro-symbolic reasoner based on a SAT solver to determine entailment. We evaluate our pipeline on two enthymeme datasets, demonstrating promising performance in selecting the correct implicit premise, as measured by precision, recall, F1-score, and accuracy.

zh.title: 在省略论证的逻辑理解中明确隐含前提
zh.summary: 一个结合大语言模型和神经符号推理的管道识别和形式化省略论证中的隐含前提，能够自动检测现实话语中的逻辑蕴含。

fr.title: Rendre explicites les prémisses implicites dans la compréhension logique des enthymèmes
fr.summary: Un pipeline combinant des LLM et un raisonnement neuro-symbolique identifie et formalise les prémisses implicites dans les arguments enthymématiques, permettant la détection automatique de l'implication logique dans le discours réel.

de.title: Explizitmachen impliziter Prämissen zum logischen Verständnis von Enthymemen
de.summary: Ein Pipeline-System, das LLMs und neuro-symbolisches Denken kombiniert, identifiziert und formalisiert implizite Prämissen in enthymematischen Argumenten und ermöglicht die automatische Erkennung logischer Entailment in realen Diskursen.

es.title: Hacer explícitas las premisas implícitas en la comprensión lógica de entimemas
es.summary: Un pipeline que combina LLMs y razonamiento neuro-simbólico identifica y formaliza premisas implícitas en argumentos entimemáticos, permitiendo la detección automática de implicación lógica en el discurso real.

title: A Hazard-Informed Data Pipeline for Robotics Physical Safety
summary: This work presents a framework combining classical risk engineering with machine learning for robotic safety, using synthetic data generation and a formalized hazard ontology. It bridges digital twins and machine learning to enable safety envelope learning for autonomous systems.
content:
arXiv:2603.06130v1 Announce Type: cross 
Abstract: This report presents a structured Robotics Physical Safety Framework based on explicit asset declaration, systematic vulnerability enumeration, and hazard-driven synthetic data generation. The approach bridges classical risk engineering with modern machine learning pipelines, enabling safety envelope learning grounded in a formalized hazard ontology. The key contribution of this framework is the alignment between classical safety engineering, digital twin simulation, synthetic data generation, and machine learning model training.

zh.title: 面向机器人物理安全的危害信息化数据管道
zh.summary: 该工作提出了一个框架，将经典风险工程与机器学习相结合，用于机器人安全，采用合成数据生成和形式化的危害本体。它连接了数字孪生和机器学习，使自主系统能够进行安全包络学习。

fr.title: Un pipeline de données informé par les risques pour la sécurité physique de la robotique
fr.summary: Ce travail présente un cadre combinant l'ingénierie des risques classique avec l'apprentissage automatique pour la sécurité des robots, en utilisant la génération de données synthétiques et une ontologie des risques formalisée. Il relie les jumeaux numériques et l'apprentissage automatique pour permettre l'apprentissage d'enveloppes de sécurité pour les systèmes autonomes.

de.title: Eine risikoinformierte Datenpipeline für die physische Sicherheit der Robotik
de.summary: Diese Arbeit präsentiert ein Framework, das klassische Risikoentwicklung mit maschinellem Lernen für Robotersicherheit kombiniert und synthetische Datenerzeugung sowie eine formalisierte Hazard-Ontologie nutzt. Sie verbindet digitale Zwillinge und maschinelles Lernen, um das Lernen von Sicherheitshüllen für autonome Systeme zu ermöglichen.

es.title: Una canalización de datos informada sobre riesgos para la seguridad física de la robótica
es.summary: Este trabajo presenta un marco que combina la ingeniería de riesgos clásica con el aprendizaje automático para la seguridad robótica, utilizando generación de datos sintéticos y una ontología de riesgos formalizada. Conecta gemelos digitales y aprendizaje automático para permitir el aprendizaje de envolventes de seguridad para sistemas autónomos.

title: A Causal Graph Approach to Oppositional Narrative Analysis
summary: Researchers propose a graph-based framework that represents narratives as entity-interaction networks to detect and classify oppositional narratives, incorporating causal estimation for interpretability. This approach outperforms existing methods by modeling structured relationships rather than linear pattern recognition.
content:
arXiv:2603.06135v1 Announce Type: cross 
Abstract: Current methods for textual analysis rely on data annotated within predefined ontologies, often embedding human bias within black-box models. Despite achieving near-perfect performance, these approaches exploit unstructured, linear pattern recognition rather than modeling the structured interactions between entities that naturally emerge in discourse. In this work, we propose a graph-based framework for the detection, analysis, and classification of oppositional narratives and their underlying entities by representing narratives as entity-interaction graphs. Moreover, by incorporating causal estimation at the node level, our approach derives a causal representation of each contribution to the final classification by distilling the constructed sentence graph into a minimal causal subgraph. Building upon this representation, we introduce a classification pipeline that outperforms existing approaches to oppositional thinking classification task.

zh.title: 因果图方法用于对抗性叙述分析
zh.summary: 研究人员提出了一个基于图的框架，将叙述表示为实体交互网络，以检测和分类对抗性叙述，并结合因果估计以增强可解释性。通过建模结构化关系而非线性模式识别，该方法优于现有方法。

fr.title: Une approche par graphe causal pour l'analyse des récits opposés
fr.summary: Les chercheurs proposent un cadre basé sur un graphe qui représente les récits comme des réseaux d'interaction d'entités pour détecter et classer les récits opposés, en incorporant l'estimation causale pour l'interprétabilité. Cette approche surpasse les méthodes existantes en modélisant des relations structurées plutôt que la reconnaissance de motifs linéaires.

de.title: Ein kausales Graphen-Verfahren zur Analyse gegensätzlicher Erzählungen
de.summary: Forscher schlagen ein graphbasiertes Framework vor, das Narrative als Entitäts-Interaktionsnetzwerke darstellt, um gegensätzliche Narrative zu erkennen und zu klassifizieren, wobei kausale Schätzung für Interpretierbarkeit integriert wird. Dieser Ansatz übertrifft bestehende Methoden durch die Modellierung strukturierter Beziehungen statt linearer Mustererkennung.

es.title: Un enfoque de gráfica causal para el análisis de narrativas opuestas
es.summary: Los investigadores proponen un marco basado en grafos que representa narrativas como redes de interacción de entidades para detectar y clasificar narrativas opuestas, incorporando estimación causal para la interpretabilidad. Este enfoque supera métodos existentes al modelar relaciones estructuradas en lugar de reconocimiento de patrones lineal.

title: Partial Policy Gradients for RL in LLMs
summary: This work introduces a policy gradient method that optimizes over subsets of future rewards, enabling learning of different policy classes from simple to complex. Testing on conversational tasks shows different policies excel at different problems, highlighting the importance of policy class selection.
content:
arXiv:2603.06138v1 Announce Type: cross 
Abstract: Reinforcement learning is a framework for learning to act sequentially in an unknown environment. We propose a natural approach for modeling policy structure in policy gradients. The key idea is to optimize for a subset of future rewards: smaller subsets represent simpler policies, which can be learned more reliably because their empirical gradient estimates are more accurate. Our approach allows for modeling and comparison of different policy classes, including full planning, greedy, K-step lookahead, and segment policies. We evaluate the policies empirically on multiple persona-alignment conversational problems. Different policies excel in different problems, reflecting their different characteristics and highlighting the importance of our studied policy class.

zh.title: 用于大语言模型强化学习的部分策略梯度
zh.summary: 该工作引入了一种策略梯度方法，在未来奖励的子集上进行优化，使得能够学习从简单到复杂的不同策略类。在对话任务上的测试表明，不同的策略在不同问题上表现更优，突出了策略类选择的重要性。

fr.title: Gradients de politique partielle pour l'apprentissage par renforcement dans les LLM
fr.summary: Ce travail introduit une méthode de gradient de politique qui optimise sur des sous-ensembles de récompenses futures, permettant l'apprentissage de différentes classes de politiques du simple au complexe. Les tests sur des tâches conversationnelles montrent que différentes politiques excellent à différents problèmes, soulignant l'importance de la sélection de la classe de politique.

de.title: Partielle Richtliniengradiente für Reinforcement Learning in LLMs
de.summary: Diese Arbeit führt eine Richtliniengradient-Methode ein, die über Teilmengen zukünftiger Belohnungen optimiert und das Erlernen verschiedener Richtlinienklassen von einfach bis komplex ermöglicht. Tests bei Konversationsaufgaben zeigen, dass verschiedene Richtlinien bei verschiedenen Problemen hervorragend abschneiden, was die Bedeutung der Richtlinienklassenauswahl hervorhebt.

es.title: Gradientes de política parcial para aprendizaje por refuerzo en LLM
es.summary: Este trabajo introduce un método de gradiente de política que optimiza sobre subconjuntos de recompensas futuras, permitiendo el aprendizaje de diferentes clases de políticas de simple a complejo. Las pruebas en tareas conversacionales muestran que diferentes políticas sobresalen en diferentes problemas, destacando la importancia de la selección de clase de política.

title: Place-it-R1: Unlocking Environment-aware Reasoning Potential of MLLM for Video Object Insertion
summary: This paper presents an end-to-end framework for physically plausible video object insertion that leverages multimodal LLMs for environment-aware reasoning. The system combines chain-of-thought reasoning with iterative diffusion refinement to achieve physically coherent edits with user-controlled plausibility-fidelity trade-offs.
content:
arXiv:2603.06140v1 Announce Type: cross 
Abstract: Modern video editing techniques have achieved high visual fidelity when inserting video objects. However, they focus on optimizing visual fidelity rather than physical causality, leading to edits that are physically inconsistent with their environment. In this work, we present Place-it-R$1$, an end-to-end framework for video object insertion that unlocks the environment-aware reasoning potential of Multimodal Large Language Models (MLLMs). Our framework leverages the Chain-of-Thought (CoT) reasoning of MLLMs to orchestrate video diffusion, following a Think-then-Place paradigm. To bridge cognitive reasoning and generative execution, we introduce three key innovations: First, MLLM performs physical scene understanding and interaction reasoning, generating environment-aware chain-of-thought tokens and inferring valid insertion regions to explicitly guide the diffusion toward physically plausible insertion. Then, we introduce MLLM-guided Spatial Direct Preference Optimization (DPO), where diffusion outputs are fed back to the MLLM for scoring, enabling visual naturalness. During inference, the MLLM iteratively triggers refinement cycles and elicits adaptive adjustments from the diffusion model, forming a closed-loop that progressively enhances editing quality. Furthermore, we provide two user-selectable modes: a plausibility-oriented flexible mode that permits environment modifications (\eg, generating support structures) to enhance physical plausibility, and a fidelity-oriented standard mode that preserves scene integrity for maximum fidelity, offering users explicit control over the plausibility-fidelity trade-off. Extensive experiments demonstrate Place-it-R1 achieves physically-coherent video object insertion compared with state-of-the-art solutions and commercial models.

zh.title: Place-it-R1：释放MLLM在视频对象插入中的环境感知推理潜能
zh.summary: 本文提出了一个端到端框架，用于物理上可信的视频对象插入，利用多模态大语言模型进行环境感知推理。该系统将思维链推理与迭代扩散精化相结合，以实现物理上一致的编辑，并具有用户可控的可信度-保真度权衡。

fr.title: Place-it-R1 : Libérer le potentiel de raisonnement conscient de l'environnement des MLLM pour l'insertion d'objets vidéo
fr.summary: Cet article présente un cadre bout en bout pour l'insertion d'objets vidéo physiquement plausible qui exploite les LLM multimodaux pour le raisonnement conscient de l'environnement.
