Le système combine le raisonnement en chaîne de pensée avec le raffinement itératif par diffusion pour obtenir des modifications physiquement cohérentes avec des compromis de plausibilité-fidélité contrôlés par l'utilisateur.

de.title: Place-it-R1: Erschließung des umweltbewussten Reasoning-Potenzials von MLLM für die Videoobjektinsertion
de.summary: Dieses Papier präsentiert ein End-to-End-Framework für physikalisch plausible Videoobjektinsertion, das multimodale LLMs für umweltbewusstes Reasoning nutzt. Das System kombiniert Chain-of-Thought-Reasoning mit iterativer Diffusionsverfeinerung, um physikalisch kohärente Bearbeitungen mit von Benutzer kontrollierten Plausibilität-Treue-Kompromissen zu erzielen.

es.title: Place-it-R1: Desbloquear el potencial de razonamiento consciente del entorno de MLLM para la inserción de objetos de vídeo
es.summary: Este documento presenta un marco de extremo a extremo para la inserción de objetos de vídeo físicamente plausible que aprovecha los LLM multimodales para el razonamiento consciente del entorno. El sistema combina el razonamiento de cadena de pensamiento con el refinamiento iterativo de difusión para lograr ediciones físicamente coherentes con compensaciones de plausibilidad-fidelidad controladas por el usuario.

title: Predictive Coding Graphs are a Superset of Feedforward Neural Networks
summary: Researchers prove that predictive coding graphs mathematically encompass feedforward neural networks, positioning this neuroscience-inspired model within contemporary machine learning. This finding strengthens the theoretical case for studying non-hierarchical network topologies in ML.
content:
arXiv:2603.06142v1 Announce Type: cross 
Abstract: Predictive coding graphs (PCGs) are a recently introduced generalization to predictive coding networks, a neuroscience-inspired probabilistic latent variable model. Here, we prove how PCGs define a mathematical superset of feedforward artificial neural networks (multilayer perceptrons). This positions PCNs more strongly within contemporary machine learning (ML), and reinforces earlier proposals to study the use of non-hierarchical neural networks for ML tasks, and more generally the notion of topology in neural networks.

zh.title: 预测编码图是前馈神经网络的超集
zh.summary: 研究人员证明了预测编码图在数学上包含前馈神经网络，将这一神经科学启发的模型定位在当代机器学习中。这一发现加强了在机器学习中研究非层次性网络拓扑的理论依据。

fr.title: Les graphes de codage prédictif sont un surensemble des réseaux de neurones à rétroaction
fr.summary: Les chercheurs prouvent que les graphes de codage prédictif englobent mathématiquement les réseaux de neurones à rétroaction, positionnant ce modèle inspiré des neurosciences au sein de l'apprentissage automatique contemporain. Cette découverte renforce le cas théorique pour l'étude des topologies de réseau non hiérarchiques en apprentissage automatique.

de.title: Vorhersage-Coding-Graphen sind eine Obermenge von Feedforward-Neuronalen Netzen
de.summary: Forscher beweisen, dass Vorhersage-Coding-Graphen mathematisch Feedforward-Neuronale Netze umfassen und positionieren dieses von Neurowissenschaften inspirierte Modell in zeitgenössischem maschinellem Lernen. Dieser Fund stärkt den theoretischen Fall für die Untersuchung nicht-hierarchischer Netzwerk-Topologien in ML.

es.title: Los gráficos de codificación predictiva son un superconjunto de redes neuronales de retroalimentación
es.summary: Los investigadores demuestran que los gráficos de codificación predictiva abarcan matemáticamente las redes neuronales de retroalimentación, posicionando este modelo inspirado en las neurociencias dentro del aprendizaje automático contemporáneo. Este hallazgo fortalece el argumento teórico para estudiar topologías de red no jerárquicas en ML.

title: VLM-RobustBench: A Comprehensive Benchmark for Robustness of Vision-Language Models
summary: This benchmark evaluates vision-language models across 133 corrupted settings including noise, blur, weather, and geometric perturbations, revealing that spatial distortions degrade performance more severely than visually obvious corruptions. The findings suggest current VLMs are semantically strong but spatially fragile.
content:
arXiv:2603.06148v1 Announce Type: cross 
Abstract: Vision-language models (VLMs) achieve strong performance on standard, high-quality datasets, but we still do not fully understand how they perform under real-world image distortions. We present VLM-RobustBench, a benchmark spanning 49 augmentation types across noise, blur, weather, digital, and geometric perturbations, evaluated under graded severities (low/mid/high) and binary transforms, yielding 133 corrupted settings. We evaluate VLMs from four families (Qwen, InternVL, Molmo, Gemma) on two complementary benchmarks: MMBench (visually grounded) and MMMU-Pro (reasoning-oriented). Our results reveal that visual severity is a weak predictor of difficulty: low-severity spatial perturbations often degrade performance more than visually severe photometric corruptions. In particular, low-severity glass_blur reduces MMBench accuracy by about 8 pp on average across models, while the largest drops arise from resampling and geometric distortions (e.g., upsample, elastic_transform), reaching up to 34 pp. Overall, our findings suggest current VLMs are semantically strong but spatially fragile, motivating the definition of novel robustness evaluation protocols and training regimes that emphasize resampling and geometric invariances.

zh.title: VLM-RobustBench：视觉语言模型鲁棒性的综合基准
zh.summary: 该基准在133个损坏设置中评估了视觉语言模型，包括噪声、模糊、天气和几何扰动，揭示了空间扭曲对性能的影响比视觉上明显的损坏更严重。研究结果表明当前的视觉语言模型在语义上强大但在空间上脆弱。

fr.title: VLM-RobustBench : Un benchmark complet pour la robustesse des modèles vision-langage
fr.summary: Ce benchmark évalue les modèles vision-langage dans 133 paramètres corrompus incluant le bruit, le flou, la météo et les perturbations géométriques, révélant que les distorsions spatiales dégradent les performances plus sévèrement que les corruptions visuellement évidentes. Les résultats suggèrent que les VLM actuels sont sémantiquement forts mais spatialement fragiles.

de.title: VLM-RobustBench: Ein umfassendes Benchmark für die Robustheit von Vision-Language-Modellen
de.summary: Dieses Benchmark bewertet Vision-Language-Modelle über 133 korruptierte Einstellungen hinweg, einschließlich Rauschen, Unschärfe, Wetter und geometrischer Störungen, und zeigt, dass räumliche Verzerrungen die Leistung schwerwiegender beeinträchtigen als visuell offensichtliche Korruptionen. Die Ergebnisse deuten darauf hin, dass aktuelle VLMs semantisch stark aber räumlich fragil sind.

es.title: VLM-RobustBench: Un punto de referencia integral para la robustez de los modelos de visión-lenguaje
es.summary: Este punto de referencia evalúa los modelos de visión-lenguaje en 133 configuraciones corruptas que incluyen ruido, desenfoque, clima y perturbaciones geométricas, revelando que las distorsiones espaciales degradan el rendimiento más severamente que las corrupciones visualmente obvias. Los hallazgos sugieren que los VLM actuales son semánticamente fuertes pero espacialmente frágiles.

title: Ensemble Graph Neural Networks for Probabilistic Sea Surface Temperature Forecasting via Input Perturbations
summary: This work applies ensemble graph neural networks to regional ocean forecasting by introducing diversity through input perturbations rather than retraining multiple models. Results show spatially coherent perturbation structures improve calibration and uncertainty representation without additional training costs.
content:
arXiv:2603.06153v1 Announce Type: cross 
Abstract: Accurate regional ocean forecasting requires models that are both computationally efficient and capable of representing predictive uncertainty. This work investigates ensemble learning strategies for sea surface temperature (SST) forecasting using Graph Neural Networks (GNNs), with a focus on how input perturbation design affects forecast skill and uncertainty representation. We adapt a GNN architecture to the Canary Islands region in the North Atlantic and implement a homogeneous ensemble approach inspired by bagging, where diversity is introduced during inference by perturbing initial ocean states rather than retraining multiple models. Several noise-based ensemble generation strategies are evaluated, including Gaussian noise, Perlin noise, and fractal Perlin noise, with systematic variation of noise intensity and spatial structure. Ensemble forecasts are assessed over a 15-day horizon using deterministic metrics (RMSE and bias) and probabilistic metrics, including the Continuous Ranked Probability Score (CRPS) and the Spread-skill ratio. Results show that, while deterministic skill remains comparable to the single-model forecast, the type and structure of input perturbations strongly influence uncertainty representation, particularly at longer lead times. Ensembles generated with spatially coherent perturbations, such as low-resolution Perlin noise, achieve better calibration and lower CRPS than purely random Gaussian perturbations. These findings highlight the critical role of noise structure and scale in ensemble GNN design and demonstrate that carefully constructed input perturbations can yield well-calibrated probabilistic forecasts without additional training cost, supporting the feasibility of ensemble GNNs for operational regional ocean prediction.

zh.title: 用于概率海表温度预报的集合图神经网络
zh.summary: 这项工作通过输入扰动而不是重新训练多个模型,将集合图神经网络应用于区域海洋预报。结果表明,空间相干的扰动结构在不增加额外训练成本的情况下,改善了校准和不确定性表示。

fr.title: Réseaux de neurones graphiques d'ensemble pour la prévision probabiliste de la température de surface de la mer via les perturbations d'entrée
fr.summary: Ce travail applique les réseaux de neurones graphiques d'ensemble aux prévisions océaniques régionales en introduisant la diversité par des perturbations d'entrée plutôt que par le réapprentissage de plusieurs modèles. Les résultats montrent que les structures de perturbation spatialement cohérentes améliorent l'étalonnage et la représentation de l'incertitude sans coûts d'entraînement supplémentaires.

de.title: Ensemble-Graphenneuralnetze für probabilistische Vorhersage der Meeresoberflächentemperatur durch Eingabestörungen
de.summary: Diese Arbeit wendet Ensemble-Graphenneuralnetze auf regionale Ozeanvorhersagen an, indem sie Vielfalt durch Eingabestörungen statt durch erneutes Training mehrerer Modelle einführt. Die Ergebnisse zeigen, dass räumlich kohärente Störungsstrukturen die Kalibrierung und Unsicherheitsdarstellung ohne zusätzliche Trainingskosten verbessern.

es.title: Redes Neurales Gráficas de Conjunto para Pronóstico Probabilístico de Temperatura de Superficie Marina mediante Perturbaciones de Entrada
es.summary: Este trabajo aplica redes neurales gráficas de conjunto a la predicción oceánica regional introduciendo diversidad mediante perturbaciones de entrada en lugar de reentrenar múltiples modelos. Los resultados muestran que las estructuras de perturbación espacialmente coherentes mejoran la calibración y la representación de incertidumbre sin costos de entrenamiento adicionales.

title: Do Compact SSL Backbones Matter for Audio Deepfake Detection? A Controlled Study with RAPTOR
summary: This controlled study shows that multilingual HuBERT pre-training drives cross-domain robustness in audio deepfake detection, enabling 100M models to match larger commercial systems. Results indicate SSL pre-training trajectory, not model scale, is the primary factor for reliable detection.
content:
arXiv:2603.06164v1 Announce Type: cross 
Abstract: Self-supervised learning (SSL) underpins modern audio deepfake detection, yet most prior work centers on a single large wav2vec2-XLSR backbone, leaving compact under studied. We present RAPTOR, Representation Aware Pairwise-gated Transformer for Out-of-domain Recognition a controlled study of compact SSL backbones from the HuBERT and WavLM within a unified pairwise-gated fusion detector, evaluated across 14 cross-domain benchmarks. We show that multilingual HuBERT pre-training is the primary driver of cross-domain robustness, enabling 100M models to match larger and commercial systems. Beyond EER, we introduce a test-time augmentation protocol with perturbation-based aleatoric uncertainty to expose calibration differences invisible to standard metrics: WavLM variants exhibit overconfident miscalibration under perturbation, whereas iterative mHuBERT remains stable. These findings indicate that SSL pre-training trajectory, not model scale, drives reliable audio deepfake detection.

zh.title: 紧凑型SSL骨干网络对音频深度伪造检测是否重要？RAPTOR受控研究
zh.summary: 这项受控研究表明,多语言HuBERT预训练推动了音频深度伪造检测中的跨域鲁棒性,使100M模型能够与更大的商业系统相匹配。结果表明,SSL预训练轨迹而非模型规模是可靠检测的主要因素。

fr.title: Les backbones SSL compacts sont-ils importants pour la détection de deepfakes audio ? Une étude contrôlée avec RAPTOR
fr.summary: Cette étude contrôlée montre que la pré-entraînement HuBERT multilingue favorise la robustesse inter-domaines dans la détection de deepfakes audio, permettant aux modèles de 100M de correspondre aux systèmes commerciaux plus grands. Les résultats indiquent que la trajectoire de pré-entraînement SSL, et non l'échelle du modèle, est le facteur principal pour une détection fiable.

de.title: Sind kompakte SSL-Backbones für die Audio-Deepfake-Erkennung wichtig? Eine kontrollierte Studie mit RAPTOR
de.summary: Diese kontrollierte Studie zeigt, dass multilinguales HuBERT-Vortraining Cross-Domain-Robustheit bei der Audio-Deepfake-Erkennung fördert und 100-M-Modellen ermöglicht, mit größeren kommerziellen Systemen mithalten zu können. Die Ergebnisse deuten darauf hin, dass die SSL-Vortrainingstrajectorie und nicht die Modellgröße der Hauptfaktor für zuverlässige Erkennung ist.

es.title: ¿Importan las columnas vertebrales SSL compactas para la detección de deepfakes de audio? Un estudio controlado con RAPTOR
es.summary: Este estudio controlado muestra que el preentrenamiento multilingüe de HuBERT impulsa la robustez entre dominios en la detección de deepfakes de audio, permitiendo que los modelos de 100M coincidan con sistemas comerciales más grandes. Los resultados indican que la trayectoria de preentrenamiento SSL, no la escala del modelo, es el factor principal para una detección confiable.

title: Reflective Flow Sampling Enhancement
summary: Researchers propose RF-Sampling, a training-free enhancement framework designed for flow matching text-to-image models like FLUX that improves generation quality and prompt alignment. The method performs gradient ascent on text-image alignment by leveraging text representation combinations and flow inversion.
content:
arXiv:2603.06165v1 Announce Type: cross 
Abstract: The growing demand for text-to-image generation has led to rapid advances in generative modeling. Recently, text-to-image diffusion models trained with flow matching algorithms, such as FLUX, have achieved remarkable progress and emerged as strong alternatives to conventional diffusion models. At the same time, inference-time enhancement strategies have been shown to improve the generation quality and text-prompt alignment of text-to-image diffusion models. However, these techniques are mainly applicable to conventional diffusion models and usually fail to perform well on flow models. To bridge this gap, we propose Reflective Flow Sampling (RF-Sampling), a theoretically-grounded and training-free inference enhancement framework explicitly designed for flow models, especially for the CFG-distilled variants (i.e., models distilled from CFG guidance techniques), like FLUX. Departing from heuristic interpretations, we provide a formal derivation proving that RF-Sampling implicitly performs gradient ascent on the text-image alignment score. By leveraging a linear combination of textual representations and integrating them with flow inversion, RF-Sampling allows the model to explore noise spaces that are more consistent with the input prompt. Extensive experiments across multiple benchmarks demonstrate that RF-Sampling consistently improves both generation quality and prompt alignment. Moreover, RF-Sampling is also the first inference enhancement method that can exhibit test-time scaling ability to some extent on FLUX.

zh.title: 反射流采样增强
zh.summary: 研究人员提出了RF-Sampling,这是一个为流匹配文本到图像模型(如FLUX)设计的无需训练的增强框架,可提高生成质量和提示对齐度。该方法通过利用文本表示组合和流反演,对文本图像对齐执行梯度上升。

fr.title: Amélioration de l'échantillonnage par flux réfléchi
fr.summary: Les chercheurs proposent RF-Sampling, un cadre d'amélioration sans entraînement conçu pour les modèles de correspondance de flux texte-image comme FLUX qui améliore la qualité de génération et l'alignement des invites. La méthode effectue une ascension de gradient sur l'alignement texte-image en exploitant les combinaisons de représentation textuelle et l'inversion de flux.

de.title: Verbesserung der Reflexionsfluss-Abtastung
de.summary: Forscher schlagen RF-Sampling vor, ein trainingsfreies Verbesserungsframework für Flow-Matching-Text-zu-Bild-Modelle wie FLUX, das die Generierungsqualität und Aufforderungsausrichtung verbessert. Die Methode führt Gradientenanstieg bei der Text-Bild-Ausrichtung durch, indem sie Textkombinationen und Flow-Inversion nutzt.

es.title: Mejora del Muestreo de Flujo Reflexivo
es.summary: Los investigadores proponen RF-Sampling, un marco de mejora sin entrenamiento diseñado para modelos de flujo coincidente de texto a imagen como FLUX que mejora la calidad de generación y la alineación de indicaciones. El método realiza ascenso de gradiente en la alineación de texto-imagen aprovechando combinaciones de representación de texto e inversión de flujo.

title: Contrastive-to-Self-Supervised: A Two-Stage Framework for Script Similarity Learning
summary: This work proposes a two-stage framework using contrastive learning on labeled alphabets followed by teacher-student distillation for script similarity learning on historical writing systems. The approach enables both discriminative boundaries between systems and soft similarities reflecting potential historical connections.
content:
arXiv:2603.06180v1 Announce Type: cross 
Abstract: Learning similarity metrics for glyphs and writing systems faces a fundamental challenge: while individual graphemes within invented alphabets can be reliably labeled, the historical relationships between different scripts remain uncertain and contested. We propose a two-stage framework that addresses this epistemological constraint. First, we train an encoder with contrastive loss on labeled invented alphabets, establishing a teacher model with robust discriminative features. Second, we extend to historically attested scripts through teacher-student distillation, where the student learns unsupervised representations guided by the teacher's knowledge but free to discover latent cross-script similarities. The asymmetric setup enables the student to learn deformation-invariant embeddings while inheriting discriminative structure from clean examples. Our approach bridges supervised contrastive learning and unsupervised discovery, enabling both hard boundaries between distinct systems and soft similarities reflecting potential historical influences. Experiments on diverse writing systems demonstrate effective few-shot glyph recognition and meaningful script clustering without requiring ground-truth evolutionary relationships.

zh.title: 对比到自监督：脚本相似性学习的两阶段框架
zh.summary: 这项工作提出了一个两阶段框架，在标记的字母上使用对比学习，随后进行师生蒸馏，用于历史书写系统的脚本相似性学习。该方法既能实现系统之间的判别性边界，也能反映潜在历史联系的软相似性。

fr.title: Du Contrastif à l'Auto-Supervisé : Un Cadre à Deux Étapes pour l'Apprentissage de la Similarité des Scripts
fr.summary: Ce travail propose un cadre à deux étapes utilisant l'apprentissage contrastif sur des alphabets étiquetés suivi d'une distillation enseignant-étudiant pour l'apprentissage de la similarité des scripts sur des systèmes d'écriture historiques. L'approche permet à la fois des limites discriminantes entre les systèmes et des similitudes souples reflétant les connexions historiques potentielles.

de.title: Vom Kontrastiven zum Selbstüberwachten: Ein zweistufiges Framework für das Lernen von Skriptähnlichkeit
de.summary: Diese Arbeit schlägt ein zweistufiges Framework vor, das kontrastives Lernen an gekennzeichneten Alphabeten gefolgt von Lehrer-Schüler-Destillation für das Lernen von Skriptähnlichkeit an historischen Schreibsystemen verwendet. Der Ansatz ermöglicht sowohl diskriminative Grenzen zwischen Systemen als auch sanfte Ähnlichkeiten, die potenzielle historische Verbindungen widerspiegeln.

es.title: De Contrastivo a Autosupervisado: Un Marco de Dos Etapas para el Aprendizaje de Similitud de Scripts
es.summary: Este trabajo propone un marco de dos etapas que utiliza aprendizaje contrastivo en alfabetos etiquetados seguido de destilación estudiante-profesor para el aprendizaje de similitud de scripts en sistemas de escritura históricos. El enfoque permite tanto límites discriminativos entre sistemas como similitudes suaves que reflejan conexiones históricas potenciales.

title: CRIMSON: A Clinically-Grounded LLM-Based Metric for Generative Radiology Report Evaluation
summary: CRIMSON provides a clinically grounded evaluation framework for chest X-ray reports that assesses diagnostic correctness using clinical context, guideline-based rules, and a comprehensive error taxonomy with severity-weighted scoring. Validation shows strong alignment with board-certified radiologist judgments across multiple benchmarks.
content:
arXiv:2603.06183v1 Announce Type: cross 
Abstract: We introduce CRIMSON, a clinically grounded evaluation framework for chest X-ray report generation that assesses reports based on diagnostic correctness, contextual relevance, and patient safety. Unlike prior metrics, CRIMSON incorporates full clinical context, including patient age, indication, and guideline-based decision rules, and prevents normal or clinically insignificant findings from exerting disproportionate influence on the overall score. The framework categorizes errors into a comprehensive taxonomy covering false findings, missing findings, and eight attribute-level errors (e.g., location, severity, measurement, and diagnostic overinterpretation). Each finding is assigned a clinical significance level (urgent, actionable non-urgent, non-actionable, or expected/benign), based on a guideline developed in collaboration with attending cardiothoracic radiologists, enabling severity-aware weighting that prioritizes clinically consequential mistakes over benign discrepancies. CRIMSON is validated through strong alignment with clinically significant error counts annotated by six board-certified radiologists in ReXVal (Kendalls tau = 0.61-0.71; Pearsons r = 0.71-0.84), and through two additional benchmarks that we introduce. In RadJudge, a targeted suite of clinically challenging pass-fail scenarios, CRIMSON shows consistent agreement with expert judgment. In RadPref, a larger radiologist preference benchmark of over 100 pairwise cases with structured error categorization, severity modeling, and 1-5 overall quality ratings from three cardiothoracic radiologists, CRIMSON achieves the strongest alignment with radiologist preferences. We release the metric, the evaluation benchmarks, RadJudge and RadPref, and a fine-tuned MedGemma model to enable reproducible evaluation of report generation, all available at https://github.com/rajpurkarlab/CRIMSON.

zh.title: CRIMSON：一种临床接地的基于大语言模型的生成放射科报告评估指标
zh.summary: CRIMSON为胸部X射线报告提供了一个临床接地的评估框架，使用临床背景、基于指南的规则和综合错误分类法以及严重程度加权评分来评估诊断正确性。验证表明与董事会认证的放射科医生的判断在多个基准上强烈一致。

fr.title: CRIMSON : Une Métrique Basée sur LLM Cliniquement Ancrée pour l'Évaluation des Rapports de Radiologie Générés
fr.summary: CRIMSON fournit un cadre d'évaluation cliniquement ancré pour les rapports de radiographie thoracique qui évalue l'exactitude diagnostique en utilisant le contexte clinique, les règles basées sur les directives et une taxonomie d'erreurs complète avec notation pondérée par la gravité. La validation montre un fort alignement avec les jugements des radiologues certifiés par le conseil dans plusieurs repères.

de.title: CRIMSON: Eine klinisch verankerte LLM-basierte Metrik zur Bewertung generativer Radiologie-Berichte
de.summary: CRIMSON bietet ein klinisch verankertes Evaluationsrahmen für Röntgen-Thorax-Berichte, der die diagnostische Korrektheit anhand von klinischem Kontext, richtlinienbasierten Regeln und einer umfassenden Fehler-Taxonomie mit schweregrad-gewichteter Bewertung bewertet. Die Validierung zeigt eine starke Übereinstimmung mit den Urteilen von von der Facharztprüfung zertifizierten Radiologen über mehrere Benchmarks hinweg.

es.title: CRIMSON: Una Métrica Basada en LLM Clínicamente Fundamentada para la Evaluación de Informes Radiológicos Generativos
es.summary: CRIMSON proporciona un marco de evaluación clínicamente fundamentado para informes de radiografía de tórax que evalúa la corrección diagnóstica utilizando contexto clínico, reglas basadas en directrices y una taxonomía de errores integral con puntuación ponderada por gravedad. La validación muestra una fuerte alineación con los criterios de radiólogos certificados por la junta en múltiples evaluaciones comparativas.

title: Whisper-CD: Accurate Long-Form Speech Recognition using Multi-Negative Contrastive Decoding
summary: This paper presents Whisper-CD, a training-free contrastive decoding framework that improves long-form speech recognition by using acoustically motivated perturbations to guide token-level decoding. The method reduces word error rates by up to 24.3 percentage points while achieving 48% faster throughput than beam search.
content:
arXiv:2603.06193v1 Announce Type: cross 
Abstract: Long-form speech recognition with large encoder-decoder models such as Whisper often exhibit hallucinations, repetition loops, and content omissions. These errors can accumulate and be further amplified when the previous segment's transcription is used as decoding context. We propose Whisper-CD, a training-free contrastive decoding framework that contrasts clean-audio logits against negative logits computed from three acoustically motivated perturbations: Gaussian noise injection, silence signal, and audio temporal shift. We aggregate these negatives via the log-sum-exp operator, building a unified multi-negative objective for token-by-token decoding. Across five English long-form benchmarks, Whisper-CD reduces WER by up to 24.3pp on CORAAL and shows 48% faster token generation throughput than beam search. Because Whisper-CD operates purely at inference time, it can be applied as a drop-in replacement to already-deployed Whisper systems without retraining.

zh.title: Whisper-CD：使用多负对比解码的精确长形式语音识别
zh.summary: 本文介绍了Whisper-CD，一个无需训练的对比解码框架，通过使用以声学为动力的扰动来指导令牌级解码来改进长形式语音识别。该方法将词错误率降低了最多24.3个百分点，同时实现了比束搜索快48%的吞吐量。

fr.title: Whisper-CD : Reconnaissance Vocale de Forme Longue Précise Utilisant le Décodage Contrastif Multi-Négatif
fr.summary: Cet article présente Whisper-CD, un cadre de décodage contrastif sans formation qui améliore la reconnaissance vocale de forme longue en utilisant des perturbations motivées acoustiquement pour guider le décodage au niveau des tokens. La méthode réduit les taux d'erreur de mot de jusqu'à 24,3 points de pourcentage tout en réalisant un débit 48 % plus rapide que la recherche par faisceaux.

de.title: Whisper-CD: Genaue Spracherkennung für lange Formen mit Multi-Negativ-Kontrastivem Dekodieren
de.summary: Dieses Papier präsentiert Whisper-CD, ein trainingsfreies Kontrastiv-Dekodierungs-Framework, das die Spracherkennung für lange Formen verbessert, indem es akustisch motivierte Störungen zur Steuerung des Token-Level-Dekodierens verwendet. Die Methode reduziert die Wort-Fehlerquoten um bis zu 24,3 Prozentpunkte und erreicht dabei einen 48 % schnelleren Durchsatz als Beam Search.

es.title: Whisper-CD: Reconocimiento de Voz de Forma Larga Preciso Usando Decodificación Contrastiva Multi-Negativa
es.summary: Este artículo presenta Whisper-CD, un marco de decodificación contrastiva sin entrenamiento que mejora el reconocimiento de voz de forma larga utilizando perturbaciones motivadas acústicamente para guiar la decodificación a nivel de token. El método reduce las tasas de error de palabras hasta 24,3 puntos porcentuales mientras logra un rendimiento 48 % más rápido que la búsqueda de haz.

title: MAPO: Mixed Advantage Policy Optimization for Long-Horizon Multi-Turn Dialogue
summary: This work introduces MAPO, an efficient reinforcement learning algorithm for subjective multi-turn dialogue that combines dense process feedback with mixed-level advantage estimation for scalable credit assignment. Testing on emotional support benchmarks shows consistent improvements in both training stability and dialogue quality.
content:
arXiv:2603.06194v1 Announce Type: cross 
Abstract: Subjective multi-turn dialogue tasks, such as emotional support, require conversational policies that adapt to evolving user states and optimize long-horizon interaction quality. However, reinforcement learning (RL) for such settings remains challenging due to the absence of reliable process supervision. Outcome-only training collapses credit assignment across turns into a single trajectory-level reward, while na\"ive turn-level group sampling incurs prohibitive rollout costs in interactive environments. We propose a critic-free and efficient RL algorithm named MAPO that leverages dense process feedback from a judge model and propagates long-horizon effects through Monte Carlo returns. To stabilize optimization, we introduce a mixed advantage estimator that combines turn-level normalization with batch-level normalization, enabling fine-grained yet scalable credit assignment. Across multiple subjective dialogue benchmarks, including EMPA, EmoBench, and EQ-Bench, and model scales ranging from 7B to 32B, our method consistently improves both training stability and final performance over outcome-only GRPO and single-level normalization baselines. On EMPA, we improve rates by up to 9 points and increase dialogue scores by as much as +43.2 over the 7B base model. Despite training only on EMPA-style environments, our approach generalizes well, yielding consistent improvements on unseen emotional-intelligence benchmarks, including up to +4 points on EmoBench and +3.5 on EQ-Bench. Together, these results demonstrate that dense process supervision combined with mixed-level normalization enables effective and scalable RL for subjective, open-ended multi-turn dialogue.

zh.title: MAPO: 长视野多轮对话的混合优势策略优化
zh.summary: 本工作引入MAPO，一种高效的强化学习算法，用于主观多轮对话，结合密集的过程反馈和混合级优势估计，实现可扩展的信用分配。在情感支持基准上的测试显示训练稳定性和对话质量都有一致改进。

fr.title: MAPO: Optimisation de Politique d'Avantage Mixte pour le Dialogue Multi-Tours à Long Horizon
fr.summary: Ce travail introduit MAPO, un algorithme d'apprentissage par renforcement efficace pour le dialogue multi-tours subjectif qui combine les commentaires de processus denses avec l'estimation d'avantages à niveaux mixtes pour une attribution de crédit évolutive. Les tests sur les repères de soutien émotionnel montrent des améliorations cohérentes en matière de stabilité d'entraînement et de qualité du dialogue.

de.title: MAPO: Optimierung der gemischten Vorteilspolitik für längerfristige mehrteilige Dialoge
de.summary: Diese Arbeit führt MAPO ein, einen effizienten Verstärkungslernalgorithmus für subjektive mehrteilige Dialoge, der dichtes Prozess-Feedback mit gemischter Vorteilsschätzung für skalierbare Gutschriftzuordnung kombiniert. Tests an emotionalen Support-Benchmarks zeigen durchgehend Verbesserungen in Trainingsstabilität und Dialogqualität.

es.title: MAPO: Optimización de Política de Ventaja Mixta para Diálogo Multi-Turno a Largo Horizonte
es.summary: Este trabajo introduce MAPO, un algoritmo de aprendizaje por refuerzo eficiente para diálogos multiturno subjetivos que combina retroalimentación de proceso denso con estimación de ventaja mixta para una asignación de crédito escalable. Las pruebas en puntos de referencia de apoyo emocional muestran mejoras consistentes tanto en la estabilidad del entrenamiento como en la calidad del diálogo.

title: FlashPrefill: Instantaneous Pattern Discovery and Thresholding for Ultra-Fast Long-Context Prefilling
summary: FlashPrefill enables ultra-fast LLM prefilling through dynamic attention pattern discovery and a novel thresholding mechanism that avoids sorting overhead. The framework achieves 27.78x speedup on 256K sequences while maintaining 1.71x speedup on 4K contexts, making it practical across sequence lengths.
content:
arXiv:2603.06199v1 Announce Type: cross 
Abstract: Long-context modeling is a pivotal capability for Large Language Models, yet the quadratic complexity of attention remains a critical bottleneck, particularly during the compute-intensive prefilling phase. While various sparse attention mechanisms have been explored, they typically suffer from either significant search latency or insufficient sparsity. In this paper, we propose FlashPrefill, a framework enabling ultra-fast prefilling via instantaneous pattern discovery and thresholding. FlashPrefill leverages a fast block-searching technique to simultaneously locate dynamic vertical, slash, and block-sparse attention patterns. Crucially, it introduces a dynamic thresholding mechanism that bypasses the prohibitive overhead of sorting or accumulating attention scores while effectively eliminating the long-tail distribution to enhance sparsity. Extensive evaluations demonstrate that FlashPrefill achieves a substantial leap in efficiency, delivering an unprecedented 27.78x speedup on 256K sequences. Notably, unlike existing methods that incur efficiency degradation on shorter contexts, FlashPrefill maintains a 1.71x speedup even at a 4K context length, demonstrating its robustness and practical utility across varying sequence scales.

zh.title: FlashPrefill: 用于超快速长上下文预填充的即时模式发现和阈值化
zh.summary: FlashPrefill通过动态注意力模式发现和一个新颖的避免排序开销的阈值化机制实现超快速LLM预填充。该框架在256K序列上达到27.78倍加速，同时在4K上下文上保持1.71倍加速，使其在各种序列长度上都实用。

fr.title: FlashPrefill: Découverte de Motifs Instantanée et Seuillage pour un Préchargement Ultra-Rapide à Long Contexte
fr.summary: FlashPrefill permet un préchargement LLM ultra-rapide grâce à la découverte dynamique de motifs d'attention et à un mécanisme de seuillage novateur qui évite la surcharge de tri. Le cadre atteint une accélération de 27,78x sur des séquences de 256K tout en maintenant une accélération de 1,71x sur des contextes de 4K, ce qui le rend pratique sur des longueurs de séquence variées.

de.title: FlashPrefill: Momentane Mustererkennung und Schwellenwertbildung für ultraschnelles langes Kontextpre-Filling
de.summary: FlashPrefill ermöglicht ultraschnelles LLM-Pre-Filling durch dynamische Aufmerksamkeitsmustererkennung und einen neuartigen Schwellenwertmechanismus, der Sortieraufwand vermeidet. Das Framework erreicht eine 27,78-fache Beschleunigung bei 256K-Sequenzen und behält gleichzeitig eine 1,71-fache Beschleunigung bei 4K-Kontexten, was es bei verschiedenen Sequenzlängen praktikabel macht.

es.title: FlashPrefill: Descubrimiento Instantáneo de Patrones y Umbralización para Prefilling Ultra-Rápido de Contexto Largo
es.summary: FlashPrefill permite el prefilling de LLM ultra-rápido mediante descubrimiento dinámico de patrones de atención y un mecanismo de umbralización novedoso que evita la sobrecarga de ordenamiento. El marco logra una aceleración de 27,78 veces en secuencias de 256K mientras mantiene una aceleración de 1,71 veces en contextos de 4K, lo que lo hace práctico en una variedad de longitudes de secuencia.

title: Cut to the Chase: Training-free Multimodal Summarization via Chain-of-Events
summary: CoE is a training-free multimodal summarization framework that uses a hierarchical event graph to structure cross-modal reasoning and temporal modeling in videos, transcripts, and images. Extensive experiments show consistent improvements over video chain-of-thought baselines across eight diverse datasets.
content:
arXiv:2603.06213v1 Announce Type: cross 
Abstract: Multimodal Summarization (MMS) aims to generate concise textual summaries by understanding and integrating information across videos, transcripts, and images. However, existing approaches still suffer from three main challenges: (1) reliance on domain-specific supervision, (2) implicit fusion with weak cross-modal grounding, and (3) flat temporal modeling without event transitions. To address these issues, we introduce **CoE**, a training-free MMS framework that performs structured reasoning through a **Chain-of-Events** guided by a Hierarchical Event Graph (HEG). The HEG encodes textual semantics into an explicit event hierarchy that scaffolds cross-modal grounding and temporal reasoning. Guided by this structure, **CoE** localizes key visual cues, models event evolution and causal transitions, and refines outputs via lightweight style adaptation for domain alignment. Extensive experiments on eight diverse datasets demonstrate that **CoE** consistently outperforms state-of-the-art video CoT baselines, achieving average gains of **+3.04 ROUGE**, **+9.51 CIDEr**, and **+1.88 BERTScore**, highlighting its robustness, interpretability, and cross-domain generalization. Our code is available at https://github.com/youxiaoxing/CoE.

zh.title: 开门见山: 通过事件链的无训练多模态总结
zh.summary: CoE是一个无训练多模态总结框架，使用分层事件图来结构化跨模态推理和视频、转录和图像中的时间建模。广泛的实验显示在八个不同的数据集上对视频思维链基线的一致改进。

fr.title: Allez droit au but: Résumé Multimodal sans Entraînement via Chaîne d'Événements
fr.summary: CoE est un cadre de résumé multimodal sans entraînement qui utilise un graphique d'événements hiérarchique pour structurer le raisonnement intermodal et la modélisation temporelle dans les vidéos, les transcriptions et les images. Les expériences approfondies montrent des améliorations cohérentes par rapport aux lignes de base de chaîne de pensée vidéo sur huit ensembles de données divers.

de.title: Direkt zur Sache: Trainingsfreie multimodale Zusammenfassung über Ereigniskette
de.summary: CoE ist ein trainingsfreies multimodales Zusammenfassungs-Framework, das ein hierarchisches Ereignisdiagramm verwendet, um domänenübergreifende Argumentation und zeitliche Modellierung in Videos, Transkripten und Bildern zu strukturieren. Umfangreiche Experimente zeigen konsistente Verbesserungen gegenüber Video-Chain-of-Thought-Baselines über acht verschiedene Datensätze hinweg.

es.title: Directo al Grano: Resumen Multimodal sin Entrenamiento vía Cadena de Eventos
es.summary: CoE es un marco de resumen multimodal sin entrenamiento que utiliza un gráfico jerárquico de eventos para estructurar el razonamiento multimodal y la modelación temporal en videos, transcripciones e imágenes. Experimentos extensivos muestran mejoras consistentes sobre las líneas de base de cadena de pensamiento de video en ocho conjuntos de datos diversos.
